question_text: A player who has the ball and is no longer moving is ruled:
explanation: A runner who has stopped advancing is not automatically dead; the ball becomes dead when an official declares it so or a dead-ball condition (e.g., runner down by contact) occurs. This item tests the definition of a dead ball per Rule 2.

question_text: On a free kick, the kicking team may not touch the ball before it goes how many yards, unless it is first touched by the receiving team?
explanation: The kicking team is not allowed to touch a free kick before it travels 10 yards, unless the receiving team touches it first, per Rule 6.

question_text: A passer, to avoid a loss of yardage, throws a legal forward pass from outside the tackle box that lands well beyond the line of scrimmage with no eligible receiver in the area. This is:
choice_c: Legal because it crossed the line of scrimmage
explanation: Outside the tackle box, a pass must still land at or beyond the line of scrimmage; if the passer clearly could have run for positive yardage and the throw does not reach the line, it can still be grounding depending on circumstances tested here.

question_text: Targeting fouls require which type of officiating review when called on the field?
choice_c: Only the referee may overturn it without replay
choice_d: Review only if requested by the head coach
explanation: All targeting fouls are subject to instant replay review to confirm or overturn the foul, per Rule 9 and Rule 12.

question_text: A touchdown is worth how many points?
explanation: A touchdown is worth six points per Rule 8; the try afterward can add 1, 2, or 0 additional points.

question_text: When may a team substitute freely without restriction?
choice_a: Only during a team's own timeout
choice_b: Between downs, subject to the 10-second runoff rules for illegal substitution
explanation: Substitutions are generally allowed between downs, though rules exist to prevent teams from using mass substitution to conserve time illegally, per Rule 3.

question_text: A backward pass that hits the ground is:
choice_b: Still a live ball that may be recovered and advanced by either team
choice_d: Automatically a turnover to the defense
explanation: Unlike a forward pass, a backward pass (or fumble) remains live on the ground and may be advanced by either team, per Rule 7.

question_text: An official's jurisdiction over a game begins:
choice_b: When the officials arrive at the stadium
choice_c: When they enter the field for pregame duties and continues until they leave the field after the game
explanation: Official jurisdiction covers pregame through leaving the field after the game concludes, not just live-ball action, per Rule 11.

question_text: When a foul's penalty distance would place the ball more than half the distance to the goal line, the enforcement is:
choice_b: Half the distance to the goal line
explanation: Penalty enforcement is capped at half the distance to the goal line when the standard yardage would otherwise exceed it, per Rule 10.

question_text: If a targeting foul is confirmed by instant replay, what happens to the disqualified player?
choice_a: Nothing further; only the yardage penalty applies
choice_b: The player is disqualified and must leave for the remainder of the game
choice_c: The player sits one series only
choice_d: The player may return after halftime
explanation: A confirmed targeting foul results in disqualification of the offending player for the remainder of the game, in addition to the 15-yard penalty, per Rules 9 and 12.

question_text: 4Q (4:22) of the 1st game of the season, B21 is called for Targeting by the Back Judge. After video review, the call on the field is upheld.
choice_a: Team B is penalized 15 yards and B21 is ejected from the current game.
choice_b: Team B is penalized 15 yards and B21 is disqualified from the current game and will miss the first half of his team’s next game.
choice_c: Team B is penalized 15 yards with no disqualification.
choice_d: Team B is penalized 15 yards and B21 is disqualified from the current game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team B is penalized 15 yards and B21 is disqualified from the current game. First-offense Targeting carries no carryover disqualification. (Rule 9-1-3/4-Penalty; Rule 2-27-12).

question_text: 4Q (4:22) of the final game of the post-season, B21 is called for Targeting by the Back Judge. This was B21’s second Targeting foul for the season and has eligibility remaining for the following season. After video review, the call on the field is upheld.
choice_a: Team B is penalized 15-yards and B21 is ejected from the current game.
choice_b: Team B is penalized 15-yards and B21 is disqualified from the current game and will miss the first half of his team’s 1st game next season.
choice_c: Team B is penalized 15-yards with no disqualification.
choice_d: Team B is penalized 15-yards and B21 is disqualified from the current game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team B is penalized 15-yards and B21 is disqualified from the current game. This is B21's 2nd Targeting foul; a 2nd foul normally carries into the team's next scheduled game within the season, but this is the team's final game of the season, so there is no next game within the season to carry into, and Targeting disqualification never carries over to the following season. (Rule 9-1-3/4-Penalty; Rule 2-27-12).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-6. At the snap, A88 is on the end of the line of scrimmage 10 yards from the tackle position,and A84 is in the slot between the tackle and A88. Just before the Team A passer releases the ball, A88 contacts B21 three yards beyond the neutral zone. The pass is thrown to A84 who has moved in front and to the outside of the spot where A88 had contacted B21. A84 catches the pass at the B-1 and crosses the goal line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal @ B-16, Snap.

question_text: 3/20 @ 50. QB A12 rolls right, and spots receiver A88 who utilized a double move and is behind cornerback B21. A88 makes a diving catch and is down at the B-20. After the play, A88 gets up and gives a demonstrative “nose wipe” just after getting up from the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-35.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 2 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A18 and A31 to the right. Team A has two gunners, A80 and A88. These gunners are on the line of scrimmage wide and to the right of A31 with A88 nearer to the sideline. A33 is uncovered at the snap. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback.
choice_a: Team A – Illegal Formation, A33 is uncovered at the snap.
choice_c: Gunner A80 is an eligible receiver.
choice_d: Team A – Illegal Formation, gunner A80 is covered at the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A’s formation is legal. (A.R. 7-1-4 VIII).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 2 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A8 and A31 to the right. All five players are in the tackle box, and A49 has his hands above his knees. A29 is in the shield five yards behind the line of scrimmage. After this initial formation, there is a shift and A29 and A31 switch positions as A49 puts his hands on the ball. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback.
choice_b: The action is shut down after the shift, dead-ball foul for Illegal Formation.
choice_c: The formation is not set until the snapper's hands are at or below the knees.
choice_d: Team A commits a live-ball foul for Illegal Formation at the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A commits a live-ball foul for Illegal Formation at the snap. (A.R. 7-1-4 IV).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 3 X 1 formation with A33, A27, and A8 to the left of snapper A49, and A31 to the right. All five players are in the tackle box with no other teammates on the line of scrimmage within the tackle box. A49 snaps the ball to A94 who kicks the ball, and it goes out of bounds at the B-25.
choice_d: Both (a) and (b) are options for Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Both (a) and (b) are options for Team B. (A.R. 7-1-4 V).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Team A has a potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards directly behind the snapper and a three- person shield five yards deep that is not aligned within the frame of the snapper. Snapper A55 is lined up with no other linemen in the tackle box. A80 is lined up wide to the left side of the formation, and wide to the right of the snapper are A60, A65, A70, A75, and A88 who is on the end of the line. A55 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback.
choice_a: Live Ball Foul - Illegal Formation, Team A is not in a 2 X 2 formation.
choice_b: Dead Ball Foul – Illegal Formation, Team A is not in a 2 X 2 formation.
choice_d: Team A’s formation is not a scrimmage kick formation.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A’s formation is legal. (A.R. 7-1-4 IX).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-10. Team A punts and B21 completes a fair catch at the 50 after giving a valid signal. The Team B head coach elects a Fair Catch Kick. As both teams get set for the Fair Catch Kick, the receivers put #89 back in their end zone. The Fair Catch Kick is short and is caught by #89 in the end zone. He advances out to his 35-yard line.
choice_c: Receiving Team may select a Fair Catch Kick @ 50.
choice_d: Receiving Team may select a Fair Catch Kick @ their 35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Receiving Team Ball, 1/10 @ their 35. (A.R. 2-16-11 IV (b)).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-10. Team A punts and B21 completes a fair catch at the 50 after giving a valid signal. The Team B head coach elects a Fair Catch Kick. As both teams get set for the Fair Catch Kick, the receivers put #89 in their end zone. The Fair Catch Kick is short and lands 9 yards deep in the end zone and bounces over the end line untouched by the receivers.
choice_a: Receiving Team may select a Fair Catch Kick @ 50.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Receiving Team Ball, 1/10 @ 50. (A.R. 2-16-11 IV (a)).

question_text: 4Q with Team B behind by 2 points, B21 is in position to catch the punt at the 50, and he gives a valid fair catch signal. Just before the ball is touched by B21, A80 makes contact with B21, the ball then touches B21 at the 50 and is recovered by A80 at the B-45 (0:00). Team B’s coach requests a Fair Catch Kick just before the penalty is enforced.
choice_a: Fair Catch Kick @ 50, game extended for an untimed down.
choice_b: Fair Catch Kick @ A-35, game extended for an untimed down.
choice_c: Fair Catch Kick @ A-40, game extended for an untimed down.
choice_d: No option for a Fair Catch Kick, game over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Fair Catch Kick @ A-35, game extended for an untimed down. (A.R. 2-16-11 I).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-10, 4Q. Team A punts and B21 completes a fair catch at the 50 after giving a valid fair catch signal (0:35). Team A was in an illegal formation at the snap. Team B’s head coach requests a Fair Catch Kick.
choice_d: There is no option for a Fair Catch Kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Fair Catch Kick @ A-45. (A.R. 2-16-11 II (a)).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-10, 4Q. Team A punts, and B21 completes a fair catch at the 50 after giving a valid signal. The Team B head coach does not elect a Fair Catch Kick. While at the line and the QB calling signals, defense #55 jumps into the neutral zone and makes contact with the snapper. After the penalty is enforced and before the next ready for play, the head coach for the offense now requests a Fair Catch Kick.
choice_a: The offense can elect a Fair Catch Kick.
choice_b: No option for a Fair Catch Kick.
choice_c: The offense can elect a Fair Catch Kick only if Team A calls a timeout.
choice_d: The offense can elect a Fair Catch Kick if they decline the penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No option for a Fair Catch Kick. (A.R. 2-16-11 III).

question_text: FK @ A-35. A5 attempts a pooch onside kick. B55 recovers the ball at the A-42 and falls to the ground. A5 was blocked aggressively by B22 at the A-38 before the kick touched the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, FK @ 50. (Rule 9-1-16-c).

question_text: FK following a safety @ A-20. Kicker A5 is standing on the A-17 right hash mark preparing to execute a punt. After the RFP, A5 sprints to his left and punts the ball from the A-18 left hash mark. The kick is caught by B33 at the B-40 following his fair catch signal.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, FK @ A-15, dead-ball foul.

question_text: 3/20 @ A-35, 2Q. Score A-17, B-21. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A10's pass is complete to A37 at the A-40, and he runs to the 50 where he is hit hard by B19, bringing him to the ground (0:34). The Back Judge has a flag for Targeting on B19. Video shows that B19 launched, and leading with his shoulder, made forcible contact to A37's head / neck area.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-35, Snap at Team A's Option (0:34).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5 @ 50, Ready (0:24).

question_text: B55 commits a taunting foul in the 3Q. This is B55’s second unsportsmanlike conduct foul of the game.
choice_a: B55 is ejected from the game.
choice_b: B55 will have a first-half suspension for the next game but can participate in pre-game warm-up activities.
choice_c: B55 must remain out of view of the field of play during the first half of the next game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B55 is ejected from the game.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-30. A2's FG attempt is wide left. During the kick, B75 twists and turns A68's face mask at the B-28.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-14.

question_text: 2/8 @ B-18. B44 intercepts Team A’s pass at the B-4, and his momentum carries him into the EZ. He is hit and fumbles while still in the EZ. The loose ball rolls into the field of play and is recovered by grounded B55 at the B-2. While the ball was loose following the fumble, B22 committed a blocking below the waist foul at the B-6.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 pts for Team A.

question_text: FK @ A-35. A33 races down the sideline to cover the kick. Just after B88 receives the ball and starts his return, A33 steps on the sideline. He then turns toward the middle of the field and is one of several players that tackle B88 at the B-23.
choice_d: Both (b) and (c) are options for Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-23.

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20, 2Q. Score A-14, B-10. A6 carries to the A-25 where he is hit by B98 (1:26) and fumbles. A87 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the A-30 (1:23), and the clock continues to run. Just prior to the next snap, Replay stops the game to review the previous play for potential Targeting. Video shows that B98 lowered his head and attacked A6 with forcible contact using the crown of the helmet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-40, Ready (1:23).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A6's kick is blocked. The loose ball is muffed several times but is eventually recovered by B54 and carried across A's goal line. While the ball was loose after the block, B96 blocked A78 below the waist.
choice_d: No foul. Two points for B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No score. A, Try @ B-11⁄2. (Rule 9-1-6-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A8's pass is intercepted by B24 and returned across A's goal line. During the return, the Line Judge ran into a Team B assistant coach who was "in the white."
choice_c: Two points for B. A, FK @ A-35.
choice_d: Two points for B. A, FK @ 50.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Two points for B. A, FK @ 50. (Rule 9-2-5-b).

question_text: 1/Goal @ B-10. 1st possession series of the 1st extra period. B67 is lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. A32 carries to the B-5 where he fumbles. B44 recovers the loose ball and returns it to the B-20 where A51 brings him down by attacking him forcibly with the crown of the helmet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-10. DQ A51. (Rule 10-1-4).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. 1st possession series of the 2nd extra period. Team A is in an illegal formation at the snap. A10's pass is intercepted by B31 and returned to the B-20 where he is tackled. During the return, B99 taunts A10.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ 40-yard line. (Rule 3-1-3-g-3).

question_text: Try @ B-3. 1st possession series of the 3rd extra period. A15's pass is complete to A42 for two points. B77 was flagged for roughing the passer.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, Try @ 18-yard line. (Rule 8-3-3-b-1).

question_text: 4/15 @ A-40. A2's punt is fielded by B95 at the B-25 and returned to the B-45 where he runs OOB. During the return, B97 approaches from the blind side and uses extended hands to forcibly contact A82 in the shoulder at the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-45. (Rule 9-1-18).

question_text: 4/15 @ A-40. A2's punt is fielded by B95 at the B-25 and returned to the B-45 where he runs OOB. During the return, B97 approaches from the blind side and leads with his shoulder to forcibly contact A82 in the neck at the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20. DQ B97. (Rule 9-1-18).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-35. On a sweep toward the sideline, B66 goes OOB to avoid a block. A78 goes OOB at the B-33 and blocks B66 who is still OOB at the B-31. A22 runs OOB at the B-25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/23 @ B-48. (Rule 9-1-7-c).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. A24 carries to the B-25 where he is tackled. As A79 is disengaging from his block of B55 above the thigh at the B-32, A68 blocks B55 at the knee.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/20 @ B-45. (Rule 2-3-3).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-10, 4Q. A16's pass is complete to A19 for a TD (0:00) to make the score A-27, B-28. After the pass, B78 was flagged for contacting A16 below the knees.
choice_a: Six points for A. Penalty declined by rule.
choice_b: Six points for A. Penalty may be enforced from the succeeding spot in a potential extra period.
choice_c: Six points for A. Penalty may be enforced on the try.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Both (b) and (c) are options. (Rule 10-2-5-a-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25, 2Q (1:07). A1 carries to the A-31 where he is hit and fumbles the ball forward. B99 muffs the loose ball at the A-33, and it rolls OOB at the A-29.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/6 @ A-29, Snap. (Rule 2-11-2).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-40, 4Q (1:32). From the A-38, A9 passes to A12 who is at the A-37. A12 muffs the pass, and it rolls OOB at the A-41.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/4 @ A-41, Snap. (Rule 2-11-2).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-40. From the B-42, A10 pitches back to A23 who muffs the ball at the B-44. B18 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-41 and it rolls OOB at the B-43.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-30. (Rule 9-4-1-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. A9's pass is intercepted by B37 in B's EZ where A85 twists his face mask, causing him to fumble. The loose ball is recovered by B26 while grounded at the B-2. B's best option would be:
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-15. (Rule 9-1-8-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. A9's pass is intercepted by B37 in B's EZ where A85 twists his face mask, causing him to fumble. The loose ball rolls OOB at the B-2. B's best option would be:
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-35. (Rule 9-1-8-b).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. A6's pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-4, and his momentum carries him into B's EZ. From there, he fumbles and the loose ball is recovered by B35 while grounded at the B-2. After the interception, B17 blocked A86 in the back at the B-6.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety. Two points for A. (Rule 9-3-5).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-30. A6's pass is intercepted by B19 at the B-4, and his momentum carries him into B's EZ. From there, he fumbles and the loose ball rolls OOB at the B-2. After the interception, B17 blocked A86 in the back at the B-6.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-2. (Rule 9-3-5).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. A5's pass is complete to A84 at the B-15. He runs to the B-6 where he is hit by B29 and fumbles. The loose ball is recovered by B34 while grounded in B's EZ. Video shows that B29 lowered his head and attacked A94 with forcible contact using the crown of his helmet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-3, Snap. DQ B29. (Rule 12-3-5-a-1).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-30, 2Q. A1 runs to near the line to gain where he fumbles with A84 recovering while grounded at the B-22 (0:57). The ruling on the field is that the fumble occurred at the B-24, and officials award a new series to Team A there. Video shows that A1 fumbled at the B-26 (0:59).
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-26, Snap (0:57). (Rule 12-3-3-f).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40, 4Q. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A5 carries to the B-31 where the ruling on the field is a fumble, recovered near the sideline by B94 (0:46). Video shows that A5 was down at the B-31 (0:48) prior to losing control of the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-31, Ready (0:38). (Rule 12-3-6-c).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40, 4Q. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A5 carries to the B-31 where the ruling on the field is a fumble, recovered near the sideline by B94. Video confirms the fumble (0:48), but shows that B94 was OOB when he first touched the loose ball (0:46).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-31, Ready (0:46). (Rule 12-3-6-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25, 3Q. A2's pass is complete to the A11 at the A-40. He carries to the B-45 where he runs OOB and B26 is flagged for a late hit. Video shows that the clock hit 0:00 before the snap. When the 4th quarter begins, it will be:
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-40. (Rule 12-3-6-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20, 4Q. Score: A-24, B-28. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A7's pass is complete to A41 where the ruling on the field is a touchdown (0:00). Video shows that A41 completed the catch, but was down at the B-1 (0:02). B96 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-1, Snap (0:02). (Rule 3-4-3-b).

question_text: FK @ A-35. The kick first hits the ground at the B-10 and is rolling towards Team B’s goal line. B33 has a knee down in the end zone when he successfully recovers the kick. Which of the following is not true:
choice_a: It is a touchback if the ball is completely behind the goal line when B33 successfully recovers.
choice_b: It is a touchback if half the ball is in the field of play when B33 successfully recovers.
choice_c: It is a safety if B33 first muffs the ball before it reaches the goal line but then successfully recovers the kick with a knee down while the ball is completely in the end zone.
choice_d: The succeeding spot is where the ball becomes dead in the field of play if B33 successfully recovers before the ball reaches the goal line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: It is a safety if B33 first muffs the ball before it reaches the goal line but then successfully recovers the kick with a knee down while the ball is completely in the end zone. (Rule 8-5-1-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. While A7's kick is in the air, B64 and B78 form a wedge at the B-20. B89 fields the kick at the B- 10 and returns it to the B-30 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-10. (Rule 6-1-10-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-15. In a formation where A8 intends to be a lineman but not at the end of the line (intentionally covered up), which of the following must be true?
choice_a: A8’s head must break the waist of the snapper.
choice_b: A8’s head must break the waist of the nearest interior lineman.
choice_c: A8’s head must break the backside of the nearest interior lineman.
choice_d: A8’s head must break the waist of the outside receiver on the line of scrimmage.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A8’s head must break the waist of the snapper. (Rule 2-27-4-a-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. After a long gain, A16 is trying to get set as a wide receiver. Before A16 gets set at the same time as his ten teammates, the ball is snapped. A16 was never in a set position. QB A11 is tackled for a loss at the B-35.
choice_a: Live ball foul for an Illegal Shift.
choice_b: Team B can decline the penalty to bring up A, 2/15@ B-35.
choice_c: Dead ball foul for a False Start.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Dead ball foul for a False Start. (Rule 7-1-2-b-5).

question_text: 4/3 @ B-12. After the Two-Minute Timeout, A19 pitches the ball from the B-13 back to A31, but A31 muffs the ball at the B-15. The ball bounces forward OOB at the B-8.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-8, Snap. (Rule 2-11-2).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. Receiver A80 lines up on the line of scrimmage, covered up by outside receiver A10. After the snap, A80 retreats into the backfield to catch an apparent backwards pass. A16 throws a pass from the A-34. A80 catches the pass at the A-35 and runs out of bounds at the A-45.
choice_a: Illegal Touching of a Forward Pass. A, 1/15 @ A-35.
choice_c: Illegal Touching of a Forward Pass. A, 2/15 @ A-35.
choice_d: Illegal Touching of a Forward Pass. A, 2/16 @ A-34.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Illegal Touching of a Forward Pass. A, 2/15 @ A-35. (Rule 7-3-3-c-1).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20, 4Q. With Team A behind in the score, Team A quickly lines up and legally snaps the ball to A12 who then throws the ball directly into the ground from the B-28 (0:09). Team A’s formation was illegal at the snap.
choice_a: Game Over. 10-second runoff is option due to the Illegal Formation foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15 @ B-25, Snap. (Rule 3-4-4-b).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-25. A16 takes that snap and rolls to his right. Linebacker B55 is at the A-32 and sees A88 on a shallow crossing route at the A-28 when B55 moves forward and pushes A88 in the back and to the ground. A16 keeps the ball and runs OOB at the A-27.
choice_a: No foul since A88 had not yet occupied the same yard line as B55. A, 3/13 @ A-27.
choice_b: Foul for Illegal Use of Hands. A, 1/10 @ A-35.
choice_c: Foul for Illegal Use of Hands. A, 1/10 @ A-37.
choice_d: Foul for Illegal Use of Hands. A, 2/3 @ A-37.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Foul for Illegal Use of Hands. A, 1/10 @ A-37. (Rule 9-3-4-e).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-45. Team A attempts a screen pass to A89, who is held at the B-49. QB A7 runs the B-42 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-32. (Rule 9-3-4-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. Team A has no timeouts remaining. After the Two-Minute Timeout, QB A12, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a pass under pressure from the B-42. RT A77 touches the ball in an attempt to catch it. The pass falls incomplete (0:54). There were no eligible receivers anywhere near the area of the pass.
choice_b: A, 4/10 @ B-40, the penalty includes an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: A, 4/12 @ B-42, the penalty includes an option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/12 @ B-42, the penalty includes an option for a 10-second runoff. (Rule 7-3-2-h).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-35. A16 muffs the shotgun snap, and it is recovered by A32 behind the line of scrimmage. A32 scrambles with the ball and three yards from the sideline, he throws a forward pass from the A-26 that crosses the neutral zone and lands OOB. A66 was at the A-41 when the forward pass was thrown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5 @ A-35. (Rule 7-3-2-h-Exception).

question_text: 3/6 @ B-44, 4Q. Score: A-21, B-21. Team A is out of timeouts. A16 scrambles as he is trying to find an open receiver downfield. He finally throws from the B-43 to A81. A81 completes the catch and is tackled in bounds at the B-19 (0:31).
choice_a: A, 4/11 @ B-49, no 10-second runoff, Team B has the option for clock status.
choice_b: A, 4/11 @ B-49, the penalty includes an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: A, 4/10 @ B-48, no 10-second runoff, Team B has the option for clock status.
choice_d: A, 4/10 @ B-48, the penalty includes an option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10 @ B-48, no 10-second runoff, Team B has the option for clock status. (Rule 7-3-2-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. Receiver B80 catches the kick in the end zone. As he begins his return, he fumbles from the end zone, and the ball rolls OOB at the B-8.
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-8. The game clock does not start during the down.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-8. The game clock starts when the loose ball enters the field of play.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-25. The game clock does not start during the down.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-25. The game clock starts when the loose ball enters the field of play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-25. The game clock starts when the loose ball enters the field of play. (Rule 3-3-2-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 3 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A8, A31, and A18 to the right. They have a gunner on each side of the field lined up on the line and three backs (including the punter) in place. Prior to the snap, Team A shifts A33 one yard back off the line of scrimmage. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback.
choice_a: Team A has committed a live-ball foul for Illegal Formation.
choice_b: Team A has committed a dead-ball foul for illegal formation.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A has committed a live-ball foul for Illegal Formation. (A.R. 7-1-4 VI (a)).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. A12 drops deep in the pocket, and A77 legally blocks edge rusher B79. At the A-35, A77 stands over B79 and brandishes a weapon. A12 then throws a pass that is complete to A88 at midfield, and he runs to the B-40 where he is pushed out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/25 @ A-25. (Rule 9-2-1-a-4 (a)).

question_text: 2/8 @ A-22. A16 completes a pass to A88 who advances to the B-45 and runs OOB. A79 verbally taunted B55 during the pass at the A-24.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/19 @ A-11.

question_text: 2/8 @ A-22. A16 completes a pass to A88 who advances to the B-45 and runs OOB. A91, a squad member on the sideline, verbally taunted B55 during the pass at the A-24.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-40. (Rule 9-2-1-a-Penalty).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-24. B30 makes an acrobatic interception. B30 is airborne when he establishes firm control of the ball at the B-3, first touches the ground with his foot when the ball is at the B-1, and then falls to the ground with sustained firm control when the ball is fully in the EZ.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20.

question_text: Try @ B-3 from the near hash. A4's pass is complete to A82 for two points; however, A17 is flagged for OPI. The Team A head coach then requests that the ball be placed in the center of the field for a one-point try.
choice_b: Request denied unless Team A calls a timeout.
choice_c: Request denied even if Team A calls a timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Request denied even if Team A calls a timeout. (Rule 8-3-2-c).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A44 sweeps right end and fumbles at the B-2. B21 recovers at the B-1 and returns to midfield and fumbles. A12 recovers at the B-48 and returns to the B-10 where he fumbles. A35 recovers at the B-5 and carries the ball across B's goal line.
choice_b: No score. Ball should be declared dead when A35 recovers.
choice_c: No score. Ball should be declared dead when A12 recovers.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No score. Ball should be declared dead when A35 recovers. (Rule 8-3-2-d-5).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. 1st possession series of the 1st extra period. A11's pass is intercepted by B23 at the B-10 and returned to the B-25 where he fumbles. A45 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ 25-yard line. (Rule 3-1-3-e).

tags: roughing the passer;blocking below the waist;2026-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. Back A41 is stationary inside the tackle box. Immediately after the snap, he steps up and blocks blitzing B98 below the waist from the front at the A-31. Quarterback A6 keeps the ball and carries to the A-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-35. (Rule 9-1-6-a-2).

tags: roughing the passer;blocking below the waist;2026-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. Guard A67 is next to the snapper. After the snap, he blocks linebacker B98 below the waist from the front at the A-83. A12 runs to the 50 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/22 @ A-23. (Rule 9-1-6-a-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-45. A31 carries to the B-30 where he is tackled. B78 lost his helmet at the line of scrimmage (no foul by A), but retrieved it, put it back on, and aggressively pursued the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Personal foul. A, 1/10 @ B-15. (Rule 9-1-17).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. A41 runs to the B-20 where B19 grabs him in the nameplate area and abruptly pulls him to the ground. Before A41 is down, he fumbles at the B-20 with A63 recovering while grounded at the B-18.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-10. (Rule 9-1-15).

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40, 4Q. Score A-16, B-17. After A1 initiates a feet-first slide with the ball at the B-30, B87 is flagged for driving his shoulder into A1's chest (0:46).
explanation: The correct ruling is: Both (b) and (c) are options. (Rule 3-4-4-a).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. A1 carries to the A-32 where he fumbles. A67 bats the grounded loose ball at the A-34 and it is recovered by A89 at the A-36.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/13 @ A-22. (Rule 9-4-1-c).

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-5. A32 runs to the B-4 where he fumbles. The grounded loose ball is at the B-2 when B76 bats it into B's EZ where A89 recovers while grounded.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety. Two points for A. (Rule 9-4-1-c).

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-5. A32 runs to the B-4 where he fumbles. The grounded loose ball is at the B-2 when B76 intentionally kicks it into B's EZ where A89 recovers while grounded.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Both (a) and (c) are options. (Rule 9-4-4).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-5. A44's punt is partially blocked and is at the A-8 when B97 bats the grounded loose ball, and it rolls OOB across the sideline in A's EZ.
choice_a: Foul. The result of the play is a safety. A, 1/10 @ A-15.
choice_b: Foul. The result of the play is a touchback. A, 1/10 @ A-20.
choice_c: Foul. The result of the play is a touchback. A, 1/10 @ A-30.
choice_d: No foul. The result of the play is a safety. Two points for B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Foul. The result of the play is a touchback. A, 1/10 @ A-20. (Rule 9-4-1-c).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-10. A21 runs toward the right sideline. As he goes airborne, the ball in his right hand crosses the sideline at the B-2. He touches the ground in the EZ with his left hand when the ball is at the B-1, and the ball crosses the goal line extended before he touches OOB.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown. Six points for A. (Rule 2-12-2).

question_text: The Team A head coach uses his first challenge on the previous play, but the ruling on the field is upheld.
choice_a: Team A has no challenges remaining.
choice_b: Team A has one and only one challenge remaining.
choice_c: Team A has one challenge remaining, and if it is successful, they will have one more challenge.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A has one challenge remaining, and if it is successful, they will have one more challenge. (Rule 12-5-1-b-1).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5. A34 runs toward the goal line pylon where the ruling on the field is a TD. Video shows that A34 lost control of the ball at the B-1, but it is impossible to tell whether the loose ball went inside, outside, or over the pylon.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal @ B-1.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. A89 establishes firm control of A7's pass while airborne at the A-33, but loses the ball when he contacts the ground with the ball at the A-35, and the pass is ruled incomplete. Video shows that after gaining control, A89 overtly extended the ball toward the line to gain. His knee contacted the ground with the ball in control at the A-34, and he lost control when his elbow hit the ground with the ball at the A-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/1 @ A-34.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. A12 carries to the B-38 where he is ruled OOB. Video shows that A12 lost control of the ball at the B-38 prior to touching the sideline, and in the immediate continuing action A89 recovered the loose ball at the B-36 and ran OOB at the B-34.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-36. (Rule 12-3-3-d-2).

question_text: FK @ A-35. The kick is long and first hits the ground beyond the end line. During the kick, B33 commits an Illegal Blindside Block (not Targeting) against A80 at the B-22.
choice_a: The penalty can only be enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The penalty can only be enforced from the previous spot. (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. Kicker A90 attempts an onside kick, and the ball goes OOB at the A-48. A81 muffed the kick at the A-44. What is the best option if Team B wants to keep the ball?
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ A-43. (Rule 6-2-1-Penalty).

question_text: FK @ A-35. B80 stands at the B-12 and gives a valid fair catch signal. B80 muffs the kick and the inflight ball is caught by teammate B49 at the B-10. Only B80 signaled for a fair catch.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-12. (Rule 6-1-5-b).

question_text: FK @ A-35. B88 fields the ball at the B-14 and runs OOB at the B-18. B91 gave a “T signal” at the B-4 during the kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-14. (Rule 2-8-3-b).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-35. Team B has 12 players in formation, and no Team B player is attempting to leave the field when the snap is imminent. QB A12, late in the play clock, is struggling to read the defense, and Team A’s Head Coach calls a timeout to avoid a Delay of Game foul.
choice_b: Team B has fouled for Illegal Substitution. Team A is charged a timeout.
choice_c: Team B has fouled for Illegal Substitution. Team A gets the timeout back.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team B has fouled for Illegal Substitution. Team A gets the timeout back. (A.R. 3-5-3 VII (a)).

question_text: 4/4 @ B-24. A8 takes the snap, pivots, and attempts to hand off to back A32 at the B-28. A32 muffs A12's handoff and the ball hits the ground but bounces into A32's hands at the B-26. A32 runs around the left end for a touchdown.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-28. (Rule 2-13-1-c).

question_text: 4/2 @ B-32. A44 takes a handoff from QB A9 and attempts to squeeze between the guard and tackle for a first down. A44 loses possession of the ball at the B-33. B16 recovers the ball at the B-27.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-27. (Rule 7-2-2-a).

question_text: 3/2 @ A-48. WR A87 releases towards the sideline to avoid contact with B10. A87 steps on the sideline at the B-48 (untouched), returns both feet inbounds at the B-42, and makes a catch of the untouched pass at the B-38. A87 advances to the B-28.
choice_a: No foul since A40 established both feet inbounds.
choice_b: A40 fouls when he returns inbounds at the B-42.
choice_c: A87 fouls when he touches the ball at the B-38.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A87 fouls when he touches the ball at the B-38.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-35. A12 throws a pass downfield and it is intercepted by B4, who runs OOB at the B-30. B90 was in the neutral zone at the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-30, Ready. (Rule 3-3-2-d-3).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30, 3Q. A5 runs to the B-28. A5 loses his helmet when he is tackled by B51 (no foul). B51 is injured on the play.
choice_a: Play clock is reset to 25 seconds.
choice_b: Play clock is reset to 40 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Play clock is reset to 40 seconds. (Rule 3-2-4-c-5).

question_text: The Center Judge is in his or her regular position after the ball is marked ready for play. Team A then quickly substitutes, gets set for the required one second, and snaps the ball. The Center Judge is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up but is unable to prevent the snap. This is the first instance of this action for Team A.
choice_a: Team A is penalized five yards for Delay of Game; Team B is allowed to complete its substitutions.
choice_b: No foul; Team B is allowed to complete its substitutions. The Referee informs the Team A head coach that any subsequent such actions will result in a foul against the team for unsportsmanlike conduct.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A is penalized five yards for Delay of Game; Team B is allowed to complete its substitutions. (Rule 3-5-2-e).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30, 4Q, clock running. Score: A-20, B-24. The ball is snapped to A16 with the intent of throwing the ball immediately forward to the ground to stop the game clock. The snap slips through A16’s hands, bounces off his facemask and high into the air. A16 moves two yards to his right, catches that ball, and immediately throws it into the ground from the B-35 (0:37).
choice_b: A, 3/15 @ B-35, the foul includes an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: A, 3/20 @ B-40, the foul includes an option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10 @ B-30. (Rule 7-3-2-f-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45, 4Q (7:00). Back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the LOS, looks to the sideline, and then goes down injured at the B-30. Both teams have all timeouts remaining.
choice_a: Team B can select a 5-yard penalty or take a timeout.
choice_b: No timeout as the ball is not ready for play.
choice_c: Officials give a warning to Team B head coach.
choice_d: If a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted, that team will be charged with a timeout and
explanation: The correct ruling is: If a player presents as injured after the ball is spotted, that team will be charged with a timeout and.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45, 4Q (7:00). Back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the LOS, looks to the sideline, and then goes down injured at the B-30. Team B is out of timeouts.
choice_a: Team B head coach gets a warning.
choice_b: If this situation occurs again, it is a Team B unsportsmanlike conduct foul.
choice_c: Since Team B is out of timeouts, they will be charged with a 5-yard delay penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Since Team B is out of timeouts, they will be charged with a 5-yard delay penalty.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45, 4Q (7:00). Back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted, B55 crosses back to his side of the LOS, looks to the sideline, and then goes down injured at the B-20 after the ball was ready for play. Team B has one timeout remaining.
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-25. No timeout charged since A21 made the line to gain. B55 must leave the game.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-25. Team B is charged with a timeout. B55 must leave the game.
choice_c: A, 1/5 @ B-20. Team B is charged with a timeout. B55 must leave the game.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-25. Team B is charged with a timeout. B55 does not have to leave the game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-25. Team B is charged with a timeout. B55 must leave the game.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45, 4Q (7:00). Back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs to the B-25 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted, B55 crosses back to his side of the LOS, looks to the sideline, and then goes down injured at the B-20 after the ball was ready for play. Team B has no timeout remaining.
choice_a: A, 1/5 @ B-20. Delay of game. B55 does not have to leave the game.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-20. No foul. B55 has to leave the game.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-20. No foul. B55 does not have to leave the game.
choice_d: A, 1/5 @ B-20. Delay of game. B55 must leave the game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5 @ B-20. Delay of game. B55 must leave the game.

question_text: Try @ B-3. First possession series of the 4th extra period. Team A previously called a timeout during the 3rd extra period. Team A substitutes late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach calls timeout just before the play clock expires.
choice_a: Timeout for Team A. A, Try @ B-3.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try @ B-8.

question_text: FK @ A-35. While the kick is in the air, B21 gives a “T signal” thinking he will not return the kick. The ball is caught by B21 at the B-5 and the officials blow the play dead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-25.

question_text: FK @ A-35. A98 kicks the ball high, and when the ball is in the air, B21 gives the “T” signal while standing 1 yard in the EZ. B21 muffs the kick from the EZ, the ball bounces forward, and then he recovers the ball at the B-5. He runs to the B-20 where he is down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-5.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Team A sends in their punt team, and has no players numbered 50 – 79. They initially line up in a scrimmage kick formation, but before the snap, back A44 shifts to a position five yards behind the snapper in the clear path from the snapper to the potential kicker. The ball is snapped to A44 who throws a legal forward pass to end A88 who catches the ball at the B-45 and runs OOB at the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/15 @ A-40.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25, 4Q (0:12). Team A is down by 4 points. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and throws the ball away beyond the NZ (0:06). The defense participated with 12 players on the field.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5 @ B-20, Snap (0:12).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25, 4Q (0:12). Team A is down by 4 points. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box, and throws the ball away beyond the NZ (0:06). The defense had 12 players on the field, but B21 was hustling to get off the field and the ball was snapped just before B21 exited the field.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5 @ B-20 - no clock adjustment (0:06).

question_text: 2/10 @ A-45, 4Q. Team A leads by 2 points. After the Two-Minute Timeout, back A21 catches a swing pass at the A-42 and runs down to the B-47 and is tackled. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the LOS, looks to the sideline, and then goes down injured at the B-30 (1:10). Neither team has a timeout remaining.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-42, No 10-Second Runoff. 40-second play clock.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-42, No 10-Second Runoff. 25-second play clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-42, 10-Second Runoff. 40-second play clock.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. A12 drops deep in the pocket, and A77 legally blocks edge rusher B79. At the A-35, A77 stands over B79 and brandishes a weapon. A12 throws a pass that is complete to A88 at midfield, and he runs to the B-40 where he is pushed OOB.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/25 @ A-25.

question_text: B55 commits a taunting foul in the 3Q. This is B55’s second unsportsmanlike conduct foul of the game.
choice_a: B55 is ejected from the game.
choice_b: B55 will have a first-half suspension for the next game but can participate in pre-game warm-up
choice_c: B55 must remain out of view of the field of play during the first half of the next game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B55 is ejected from the game. (Rule 9-2-6-a).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-30. A2's FG attempt is wide left. During the kick, B75 twists and turns A68's face mask at the B- 28.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-14.

question_text: 2/12 @ A-35. At the snap, RG A66 fires out and contacts B98 just above his waist at the LOS. He maintains contact as he slides down to A66’s thigh area. At this point, RT A77 blocks B98 also in the thigh area. This effectively neutralizes B98 as back A33 runs past him to the A-45 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2 @ A-45. (A.R. 9-1-X).

question_text: 3/14 @ A-26. A12 receives the snap and rolls out to his right. A32 runs into the side zone and turns towards A12 while standing still at the A-30. B52, sensing A12 wants to throw to A32, puts both hands into A32’s back and pushes him face-first into the ground. A12 keeps the ball and scrambles to the A-29.
choice_b: Illegal block in the back. A, 3/4 @ A-36.
choice_c: Illegal block in the back. A, 3/1 @ A-39.
choice_d: Illegal use of hands. A, 1/10 @ A-39.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Illegal use of hands. A, 1/10 @ A-39. (Rule 9-3-5).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-24. B30 makes an acrobatic interception. B30 is airborne when he establishes firm control of the ball at the B-3, first touches the ground with his foot when the ball is at the B-1, and then falls to the ground with sustained firm control when the ball is fully in the EZ.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20. (Rule 8-5-1-a).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. A10's pass is complete to A86 at the B-38 where he is tackled. Prior to the pass, B27 held intended receiver A18 at the B-32.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-35. (Rule 9-3-4-d).

question_text: 2/Goal @ B-10. Ball on the far hash. A7's pass falls incomplete off A43's fingertips. While the pass was in flight, B18 held A43 along the near sideline in B's EZ.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-2, far hash. (Rule 7-3-8-c).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. A4 muffs the snap, and A31 picks up the loose ball. He runs outside the tackle box and under duress at the A-20 throws a pass that lands at the numbers beyond the NZ in an area where there are no eligible receivers.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10 @ A-25. (Rule 7-3-2-h-Exception).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-40. A8 carries to the A-42, then retreats behind the NZ. From the A-38, he completes a pass to A29 at the 50 where he runs OOB.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/12 @ A-33. (Rule 7-3-2-e).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. A6's pass is complete to A84 at the B-15 where he is tackled. The Line Judge has a flag for A57 being at the B-20 when the pass was thrown. The Side Judge has a flag for A84 pushing off and creating separation just before the pass arrived. As the fouls are being reported, the Umpire informs the Referee that B91 tipped the pass at the B-23.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15 @ B-30. (Rule 7-3-8-a).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. A9's pass is complete to A31 at the B-42. He runs to the B-36 where he fumbles with A87 recovering while grounded at the B-38. Prior to the pass, B59 was flagged for hands to the face at the line of scrimmage.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-23. (Rule 9.1).

question_text: 1/10 @ A-35. A12's pass is complete to A24 at the A-45. He carries to the B-6 where he fumbles, and the loose ball strikes the goal line pylon. After the pass was thrown, B98 roughed A12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-3, Snap. (Rule 10-2-2-e).

question_text: 3/5 @ A-40. A43 is on the end of the line of scrimmage, covering up slot receiver A21. At the snap, both A43 and A21 drop back to potentially receive A1's pass. The ball leaves A1's hand at the A-37 and first touches A21's hand at the A-38. After the catch, A21 rushes to the 50 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10 @ A-35. (Rule 7-3-11).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-30. A11's punt is muffed by B22 at the B-25. He recovers the loose ball at the B-30 and rushes to the B-35 where he is tackled. During the kick, B44 blocked A88 in the back at the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20. (Rule 10-2-3-b).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-45. A3's punt bounces at the B-2 and is above B's EZ when A81 bats it back into the field of play. A35 then downs the ball at the B-4. During the kick, B97 blocked A68 below the waist at the B-12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-6. (Rule 10-2-3-b).

question_text: 4/15 @ B-40. A2's punt bounces at the B-3 and is above B's EZ when A46 bats it back into the field of play. A89 then downs the ball at the B-6. During the kick, A76 blocked B98 below the waist at the B-21. If Team B does not desire a re-kick, which of the following is the best option?
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-21. (Rule 6-3-11).

question_text: 4/5 @ A-20. A5's punt is first touched by A82 at the B-40. B31 fields the kick at the B-30 and carries to the B-40 where he fumbles. A41 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-45. During the kick, B16 held A32 at the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-30. (Rule 10-2-3-b-4).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-25. A13's punt is first touched by A26 at the B-35. B19 fields the kick at the B-25 and carries it to the B-35 where he fumbles. A87 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-40. During the return, B36 held A21 at the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-25. (Rule 6-3-2-b).

question_text: 4/15 @ B-30. A14's FG attempt is short and lands in B's EZ. Team A was in an illegal formation at the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-30. (Rule 8-4-2-b-1).

question_text: 4/10 @ B-25. A16's FG attempt is blocked and does not cross the NZ. After the block, the loose ball touches B98 at the B-26. A56 then muffs the ball at the B-30 before recovering it while grounded at the B-28.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-28. (Rule 8-4-2-b-2).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-20. A15's field goal attempt is partially blocked, striking the ground at the B-18 and rebounding behind the NZ. The loose ball touches B74 at the B-22 and is recovered by A63 while grounded at the B-24.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-24. (Rule 8-4-2-b-2).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-45. A31 carries to the A-47 where he fumbles. A85 bats the loose ball at the A-49, and it goes OOB at the 50.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/13 @ A-37. (Rule 9-4-1-c).

question_text: 3/15 @ A-15. From the A-13, A19 pitches back to A24 at the A-11. A31 muffs the ball, and it strikes the ground. B26 bats the loose ball at the A-12, and it goes OOB at the A-10.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5 @ A-25. (Rule 9-4-1-c).

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A17's high, short punt is almost at rest at the B-35 when A84 kicks the loose ball and it goes OOB at the B-20. If Team B does not desire a re-kick, which of the following is the best option?
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-35. (Rule 9.4).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5. A24 goes airborne near the goal line pylon and the Head Line Judge rules TD. Video shows that A24 lost control of the ball at the B-1, but is inconclusive as to whether the loose ball in flight went inside, outside, or over the pylon.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal @ B-1, Ready. (Rule 3-3-2-e-2).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-35. A4's pass is complete to A17 at the B-20 where the Field Judge has a flag for Defensive Pass Interference. Video shows that A17 stepped OOB early in his route with no contact from Team B, but immediately re-established inbounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10 @ B-35, Ready.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-30, 4Q. Score A-27, B-28. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A31 carries to near the line to gain where he is ruled to have fumbled. The loose ball is recovered by B17 at the B-24 (0:56). Video shows that A31 was down at the B-26 (0:58) prior to the ball coming loose.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-26, Ready (0:48). (Rule 12-3-6-c).

question_text: 2/15 @ A-20, 2Q. Score A-14, B-10. A6 carries to the A-25 where he is hit by B98 (1:26) and fumbles. A87 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the A-30 (1:23), and the clock continues to run. Just prior to the next snap, Replay stops the game to review the previous play for potential Targeting. Video shows that B98 lowered his head and attacked A6 with forcible contact using the crown of the helmet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-40, Ready (1:23).

question_text: 3/20 @ A-35, 2Q. Score A-17, B-21. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A10's pass is complete to A37 at the A-40, and he runs to the 50 where he is hit hard by B19, bringing him to the ground (0:34). The Back Judge has a flag for Targeting on B19. Video shows that B19 launched, and leading with his shoulder, made forcible contact to A37's head / neck area.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5 @ 50, Ready (0:24). (Rule 12-3-6-c).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-20, 4Q. Score A-31, B-35. Neither team has a timeout remaining. B97 is flagged for being offside at the snap. A8's pass is complete to A21, and he is downed just short of the goal line (0:00). Video shows that A21 was down at the B-1 with 0:01 remaining. What is the best option available to Team A?
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-1, Snap (0:01). (Rule 10-1-1-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. While A5’s kick is in flight, B88 signals for a fair catch at the B-30. The ball goes over his head and is muffed by B22 at the B-20. B33 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-14. Prior to B33’s recovery, B40 blocked A55 at the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-14. (Rule 6-5-4).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A17’s pass is intercepted in the EZ by B22. During the interception return, A34 tackles B22 by the facemask at the A-45. At the snap, B92 was lined up in the NZ.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try @ B-3. (Rule 8-3-3-a).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-17. A12’s pass is intercepted by B22 at the B-3 and his momentum carries him into the EZ. While attempting to advance the ball out of the EZ, he is hit by an opponent and fumbles. The ball rolls into the field of play where A33 attempts to recover it but muffs across the goal line and across the end line.
choice_b: Safety, 2 pts for team A. B, FK @ B-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 pts for team A. B, FK @ B-20. (Rule 8-5-1).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. A34 carries to the B-35 where he is tackled. After the snap, B17 (his team's 11th player) entered the field.
choice_c: No Foul. A, 3/5 @ A-30, Running clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Foul. A, 2/5 @ B-35, Ready. (Rule 3-5-2-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. While A7's kick is in the air, B64 and B78 form a wedge at the B-20. B19 fields the kick at the B- 12 and returns it to the B-28 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-10. (Rule 6-1-10-b).

question_text: First extra period. First possession series, 4/5 @ B-20. A5’s FG attempt is blocked behind the NZ where it is muffed by both teams. It then travels beyond the NZ where it is recovered by A44 at the B-10.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ 25-yard line, new possession series. (Rule 6-3-6-a).

question_text: 2/Goal @ B-5. A34 takes a handoff, heads to his right, and lunges towards the pylon. He is airborne as he crosses the sideline at the B-3. Before coming to the ground his left foot kicks over the pylon with the ball at the B-1, short of the goal line extended. He then hits the ground with the ball beyond the goal line extended while maintaining control of the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal @ B-3. (Rule 4-2-4-d-Exception).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. A17’s pass is intercepted by B33 who advances the ball to the A-40 where he is hit and fumbles. The loose ball is recovered by A44 at the A-45.
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ A-45, 25-second play clock, running clock.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-45, 40-second play clock, running clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-45, 40-second play clock, running clock. (Rule 3-3-2-e-1).

question_text: FK following a safety @ A-20. Kicker A5 is standing on the A-17 right hash mark preparing to execute a punt. After the RFP, A5 sprints to his left and punts the ball from the A-18 left hash mark. The kick is caught by B33 at the B-40 following his fair catch signal.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, FK @ A-15, dead-ball foul. (Rule 6-1-2-a).

question_text: Try @ B-3. A5’s placekick is touched by B21. The kick proceeds forward and hits snapper A55 in the right shoulder propelling it up and over the cross bar and between the uprights.
choice_a: One point for team A. FK @ A-35.
choice_c: No points. Illegal touching by team A, enforced on the succeeding kickoff.
choice_d: No points. Illegal touching by team A, declined by rule.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No points. FK @ A-35. (Rule 8-4-1-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. A5’s kick is muffed by B33 at the B-5. The ball rolls into the EZ where B33 chases it down and bats it across the end line.
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for team A. B, FK @ B-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, FK @ A-45. (Rule 8-6-1-b).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. 2Q, under 2 minutes. Team A is behind by 4 points. No timeouts for either team. A34 gains 5 yards. At the snap, B99 was lined up in the NZ.
choice_c: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Ready or Snap, Team A choice.
choice_d: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Ready or Snap, Team B choice.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2 @ B-32, Ready or Snap, Team A choice. (Rule 3-4-3-b).

question_text: FK @ A-35. A5 attempts an onside kick. The ball hits at the A-37 and travels to the A-42 where B55 recovers it and falls to the ground. A5 was blocked aggressively by B22 at the A-38 prior to the recovery.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, FK @ 50. (Rule 6-1-9).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. First possession series in extra periods. A17’s pass is intercepted by B44. During the return, A88 trips B44 at the B-40 and B99 taunts A22.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ 40-yard line, new series. (Rule 3-1-3-g-1).

question_text: 4/5 @ B-40. A5’s punt hits at the B-3, bounces over the goal line 1 yard into the EZ when A87, with both feet in the field of play, reaches over the goal line and bats the ball back into the field of play where A22 recovers it at the B-4.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20. (Rule 6-3-11).

question_text: 2/2 @ A-48, 4Q, under 2 minutes. Score is tied. No timeouts for either team. A17 scrambles out of the tackle box and runs to the B-43 where he tosses the ball forward and OOB at the B-40.
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-48, B accepts 10-second runoff. Ready.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-48, no option for 10-second runoff. Snap.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-43, B accepts 10-second runoff. Ready.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-48, B accepts 10-second runoff. Snap or Ready, Team B choice.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-48, B accepts 10-second runoff. Ready. (Rule 7-3-2-a).

question_text: FK @ A-35. A5’s untouched kick rolls OOB at the B-15. Which of the following is not an option for Team B?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-20. Team B may not choose the ball position between the inbounds lines.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-35. Team B may choose the ball position between the inbounds lines.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-35. Team B may not choose the ball position between the inbounds lines.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-35. Team B may choose the ball position between the inbounds lines. (Rule 6-2-1).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-12. First possession series of the 2nd extra period. A5’s FG attempt is blocked, does not cross the NZ, is recovered by A33 at the B-15, and then advanced to the B-4.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-4. (Rule 6-3-1).

question_text: 2/5 @ A-12. A17’s grounded backward pass from behind the NZ is batted forward by B33 at the A-5 and goes into the EZ and across the end line. What is the best option available to Team A?
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-22. (Rule 8-7-2-b-1).

question_text: 3/7 @ B-37. During the live-ball action, B55’s helmet comes off (not the result of a foul) and B66 is injured. By taking a team time-out, Team B has the option to:
choice_a: Keep both players in the game.
choice_b: Keep B66 in the game, but B55 must sit out one play.
choice_c: Keep B55 in the game, but B66 must sit out one play.
choice_d: Both players must sit out one play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Keep B55 in the game, but B66 must sit out one play. (Rule 3-3-6-a-1).

question_text: FK @ A-35. A33 races down the sideline to cover the kick. Just after B22 receives the ball and starts his return, A33 steps on the sideline. He then turns toward the middle of the field and is one of several players that tackle B22 at the B-23.
choice_d: Both (b) and (c) are options for Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-23. (Rule 6-1-11).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. First possession series in the 1st extra period. B33 intercepts Team A’s pass and returns it to the A-30 where he is tackled by the facemask. At the snap, B99 was lined up in the NZ, and Team A had 5 players in the backfield.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-25. (Rule 10-1-4).

question_text: 2/8 @ B-18. B44 intercepts Team A’s pass at the B-4, and his momentum carries him into the EZ. He attempts to run the ball back into the field of play but is hit and fumbles while still in the EZ. The loose ball rolls into the field of play and is recovered by grounded B55 at the B-2. While the ball was loose following the fumble, B22 committed a blocking below the waist foul at the B-6.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 pts for Team A. (Rule 8-5-1-a-Exception).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-44. Team B sends in substitute B55 to replace B52 with less than 10 seconds on the play clock. B52 races towards his team’s player area and is one stride away from the sideline when Team A snaps the ball. A34 gains 2 yards on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-39, live-ball foul. (Rule 3-5-3-b).

question_text: 2/5 @ B-35, 2Q, (0:11). RB A30 is tackled inbounds at the B-31 (0:07). Team A quickly lines up over the ball with all players set and the formation legal. The ball is snapped (0:02), and QB A1 immediately throws the ball at his feet (0:01).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-31, (0:01). (Rule 3-2-5).

question_text: Which is not a requirement for a legal snap?
choice_a: Ball must not be first moved forward or lifted.
choice_b: The ball must be controlled by the snapper from the time the ball starts backwards to when it is clearly off the ground.
choice_c: The ball must be untouched by Team B after the start of the backward movement.
choice_d: The snap need not be between the legs but must be a quick and continuous backward motion.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The ball must be controlled by the snapper from the time the ball starts backwards to when it is clearly off the ground. (Rule 2-23-1-e).

question_text: 4/16 @ A-4. Punter A99 lines up just inside of the end line prior to the snap. The snap is high. As A99 adjusts his feet to catch the snap, he steps on the end line with his right foot before reestablishing the right foot in the EZ. A99 leaps from a position with both feet in the EZ, controls the snap and lands with both feet in the EZ. A99 gets off a quick kick that rolls OOB at the A-40.
choice_d: Both (b) and (c) are options for Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Both (b) and (c) are options for Team B. (Rule 6-3-12).

question_text: What is the standard for offensive tackle A77 to be considered on the line of scrimmage?
choice_a: A77’s head breaks the plane of the line drawn through the waistline of the snapper.
choice_b: A77’s head breaks the plane of the line drawn through the rear-most body part of the snapper.
choice_c: A77’s head breaks the plane of the line drawn through the shoulders of the snapper.
choice_d: A77’s head is within one yard of the line of scrimmage.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A77’s head breaks the plane of the line drawn through the waistline of the snapper.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-41. Team A lines up with a player 11 yards behind the snapper. All 11 players are set with the following linemen in the Team A formation (from left to right): A44, A33, A56, A19, A60, A42, A28. Snapper A19 has not touched the ball and has his hands on his thigh pads. A44 then shifts into the backfield and personal protector A22 moves onto the line of scrimmage to the right of A28. A19 then places his hands on the ball and snaps to kicker A99. The kick goes OOB at the B-18.
choice_a: Illegal Formation. Numbering exceptions are established before the shift by A44 and A22.
choice_b: Legal play. Numbering exceptions are not established until the snapper places his hands on the ball or locates his hands at or below his knees.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Legal play. Numbering exceptions are not established until the snapper places his hands on the ball or locates his hands at or below his knees. (Rule 7-1-4-a-5-a).

question_text: 4/4 @ A-46. Team A is lined up in scrimmage kick formation with three linemen to the left and right of snapper A30. Which scenario is illegal?
choice_a: B55 initially lines up within the frame of the A30’s body and on the LOS. B55 drops back three yards prior to the ball being snapped.
choice_b: B55 initially lines up over the right guard and three yards off his LOS. Prior to the snap, B55 starts to move forward and is within one yard of the LOS, inside the frame of the body of A30 at the snap.
choice_c: B55 initially lines up five yards off the LOS, over the LT. B55 starts to move forward and is over the LG when the ball is snapped. B55’s next step after the snap has him still on Team B’s side of the NZ but inside the frame of the body of A30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B55 initially lines up over the right guard and three yards off his LOS. Prior to the snap, B55 starts to move forward and is within one yard of the LOS, inside the frame of the body of A30 at the snap. (Rule 6-3-14-a).

question_text: Which statement about Team B signaling is false?
choice_a: A Team B player can use a signal other than “move” or “stem” to coordinate teammate movement prior to the snap.
choice_b: A Team B player can yell “move” several times at the line of scrimmage without teammate movement prior to the snap to create a false start by Team A.
choice_c: Any signaling by Team B cannot simulate action at the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A Team B player can yell “move” several times at the line of scrimmage without teammate movement prior to the snap to create a false start by Team A. (Rule 7-1-5-a-5).

question_text: 3/2 @ A-48. Linebacker B55 verbally signals “move” and three Team B down linemen move laterally in a quick, coordinated fashion. B99, one of the down linemen, moves forward towards the NZ during his quick, lateral movement.
choice_a: B99 is legal if he clearly gets out of the NZ prior to the snap.
choice_b: B99 has simulated action at the snap. Dead ball foul for Delay of Game – Defense.
choice_c: B99 fouls only if there is a reaction from one of the protected Team A linemen over him.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B99 has simulated action at the snap. Dead ball foul for Delay of Game – Defense. (Rule 7-1-5-a-4).

question_text: 4/1 @ B-41. Two Team B linemen, B91 and B98, move in a quick, coordinated fashion upon hearing teammate B44’s verbal signal of “move.” B4, also a down lineman, has a quick, coordinated twitch of his left arm when B91 and B98 shift. B4 does not shift laterally during his actions.
choice_a: B4 has not fouled if he is not in the NZ at the snap.
choice_b: B4 fouls only if he verbally calls out a signal during his action.
choice_c: B4 has simulated action at the snap, Dead ball foul for Delay of Game – Defense.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B4 has simulated action at the snap, Dead ball foul for Delay of Game – Defense. (Rule 7-1-5-a-4).

question_text: 3/4 @ B-34. Team A is using a clap cadence. Team A lines up with all 11 players set and A88 as the Left TE and A34 as a wing back outside A88. QB A12 gives a couple of hard count claps and then reads the defense. On A12’s third hard count clap, A88 and A34 shift quickly to the other side of the formation.
choice_a: Team A has simulated action at the snap by using a quick shift in coordination with their cadence signal. This is a False Start.
choice_b: The shift of A88 and A34 is legal if they do not move forward from their set positions.
choice_c: It is a dead ball foul for Offside – Defense if a Team B player comes across the NZ in reaction to the shift of A88 and A34 and creates contact with an opponent.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A has simulated action at the snap by using a quick shift in coordination with their cadence signal. This is a False Start. (Rule 7-1-2-b-1).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. Team A is trying to line up in formation after a long gain. A81 is trying to split wide and get on the LOS. Before A81 gets set at the same time as his 10 teammates, the ball is snapped. A81 was never in a set position. QB A11 is tackled for a loss at the B-35.
choice_b: Team B can decline the penalty to bring up 2/15 @ B-35.
choice_d: Both (a) and (b) are options for Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: False Start. (Rule 7-1-2-b-5).

question_text: 2/9 @ A-31. A10 carries the ball into the side zone, trying to cut up field. B55 grabs A10 inside the shoulder pads from behind at the A-36. A10 continues to advance forward, and B55 abruptly pulls A32 down to the ground by the shoulder pads at the A-38.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-47. (Rule 9-1-15).

question_text: 2/6 @ B-46. A25 runs the ball to the B-41 and is contacted by B50. A25 is pushing forward against multiple opponents and still gaining yards. While B2 is trying to bring A25 to the ground, A66 runs up and forcibly slams into B2 from behind at the B-40. A25 is finally brought to the ground at the B-32.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15 @ A-45. (Rule 10-2-2-d-1-b).

question_text: 3/9 @ A-21. A33 takes the handoff and is running in the side zone. At the A-24, B91 initially contacts the side of A33’s helmet with an open hand and drags his open hand across the front of A33’s face mask, causing A33’s head to turn. B91’s hand never grabs the face mask or a helmet opening. A33 is tackled at the A-26.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/4 @ A-26. (Rule 9-1-8).

question_text: Which action does not create a foul for Clipping?
choice_a: Initial contact that is from behind and at or below the waist.
choice_b: Initial contact that is from behind and at or below the knee.
choice_c: Initial contact that is from the side and at or below the knee.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Initial contact that is from the side and at or below the knee. (Rule 2-5-1-a).

question_text: Which action during a FG attempt is a foul?
choice_a: B55 puts his hand on A50’s shoulder pad and gains extra height by pushing with his hand.
choice_b: B55, lined up three yards off the line of scrimmage, leaps through the gap (not in the plane above the frame of an opponent) between the snapper & LG, is blocked by A65, and then lands on A70.
choice_c: B55, lined up stationary within one yard of the LOS, leaps into the plane directly above the frame of the body of A65.
explanation: The correct ruling is: All actions above are legal. (Rule 9-1-11).

question_text: 4/4 @ A-46. Punter A99 receives the snap directly behind the snapper and moves seven yards laterally to his right and performs a rugby-style kick. B30 makes forcible contact into A99’s body clearly after the kick and before A99 moves downfield to participate in the play.
choice_a: B51 has committed a foul for Running into the Kicker.
choice_b: B51 has committed a personal foul for Roughing the Kicker.
choice_c: B51 has committed a personal foul for Unnecessary Roughness.
choice_d: B51 has not committed a foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B51 has committed a personal foul for Unnecessary Roughness.

question_text: FK @ A-35. The kick hits the ground at the B-18 and the receiving team chooses to allow the kick to roll into the EZ for a touchback. While the kick is still rolling in the field of play, B18 grabs A5 at the B-12 and forcibly drives him into the ground.
choice_c: The penalty can only be enforced from the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: The penalty can only be enforced from the previous spot. (Rule 10-2-2-d-4-a).

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. After the snap, LT A72 pulls behind the LOS and approaches DB B8 at the B-41. Seeing that he is about to be blocked by A72, B8 crouches and creates initial contact against A72’s thigh.
choice_b: B8’s block is a foul for a Block Below the Waist.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B8’s block is a foul for a Block Below the Waist. (Rule 9-1-16-b-1).

question_text: During a FG attempt where the snapper is an interior lineman, B33 is lined up in the gap between the snapper and LG. After the snap, B33 charges into the gap. The LG is pushed inside by B90, causing B33 to create contact with the snapper before one second.
choice_b: This is a personal foul for Illegal Contact Against the Snapper.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No foul. (Rule 9-1-14).

question_text: During a running play, A66’s helmet loosens through legal play, is pushed up, and is covering only half of A66’s face. A66 pushes his helmet all the way back down on his head and continues downfield to make a block for the runner.
choice_b: Personal foul on A66 for Continued Participation Without Helmet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No foul. (Rule 9-1-17).

question_text: 2/8 @ A-22. A16 completes a pass to A88 who advances to the B-45 and runs OOB. A79 verbally taunted B55 during the pass at the A-24.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/19 @ A-11. (Rule 9-2-1-a-1-b).

question_text: 2/8 @ A-22. A16 completes a pass to A88 who advances to the B-45 and runs OOB. A21, a substitute on the sideline, verbally taunted B55 during the pass at the A-24.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-40. (Rule 9-2-1-a-1-b).

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. A80 is lined up wide and to the left in front of his team’s bench. A12’s screen pass goes to the right for an 8-yard gain. During the down, A80 runs a 5-yard out pattern. After the ball becomes dead, A80 runs to a spot inside the inbounds lines at the A-30. After the ball is made Ready for Play, A80 moves to a spot in front of his team’s bench, still at the A-30 and in bounds. The remaining Team A players quickly move into formation without any substitution. All 11 players are set, including A80, and the ball is snapped. A12 throws a pass to a wide open A80, who runs to the B-5.
choice_a: Legal play since there were no Team A substitutions.
choice_b: Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul for Unfair Tactics.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul for Unfair Tactics. (Rule 9-2-2-b).
