question_text: During a long gain, the Line Judge makes contact with the Team B defensive coordinator who is standing beyond the coaching line (i.e., "in the white").
choice_b: Unsportsmanlike Conduct on Team B bench.
choice_c: Unsportsmanlike Conduct on the Team B defensive coordinator.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Unsportsmanlike Conduct on the Team B defensive coordinator. (Rule 9-2-5-b).

question_text: When the ball has become dead at the end of a down, the Back Judge notices that B44’s jersey does not cover one shoulder pad as a result of the play just completed.
choice_a: Illegal equipment. Team B is charged with a timeout and B44 must leave the game.
choice_b: Illegal equipment. B44 must leave the game but there is no charged timeout.
choice_c: Illegal equipment. B44 is not required to leave the game but the pad must be covered before the next play starts.
choice_d: Legal. As long as the shoulder pad has no rough edges, it does not need to be covered.
explanation: Illegal equipment discovered dead-ball: the player is not required to leave, but the exposed shoulder pad must be covered before the next snap. A charged timeout/removal applies only if it cannot be corrected in time.

question_text: At the end of a down, the Umpire sees that A66 is wearing black eye shade that contains a phone number written in white. Team A has no timeouts remaining in the half.
choice_a: Illegal equipment. A66 must leave the game for at least one play and may not return until the eye shade is corrected.
choice_b: Illegal equipment. The special rule for eye shade does not allow A66 to remain in the game even if Team A had a timeout available.
choice_c: Eye shade containing words or numbers is not classified as illegal equipment.
choice_d: Illegal equipment. A66 is not required to leave the game if he can fix the eye shade before the next play begins.
explanation: Eye shade with words/numbers is illegal equipment. By special rule the player must leave for at least one down and may not return until it is corrected; a timeout does not keep him in for this specific item.

tags: loose helmet; play clock; 10-second runoff; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: During a running play that ends in bounds, B55’s helmet comes off. When the ball becomes dead the game clock reads 0:45 in the second quarter.
choice_a: The play clock is set to 40 seconds and there is an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_b: The play clock is set to 25 seconds and there is not an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: The play clock is set to 40 seconds and there is not an option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_d: The play clock is set to 25 seconds and there is an option for a 10-second runoff.
explanation: B55 is a defensive player, so the play clock is 40 seconds; at 0:45 in the 2nd quarter (after the two-minute timeout) the opponent has the 10-second runoff option.

question_text: During a running play that ends in bounds, B55’s helmet comes off. When the ball becomes dead there are 10 minutes remaining in the first quarter.
choice_a: The play clock is set to 25 seconds.
choice_b: The play clock is set to 40 seconds.
choice_c: Team A may decide whether the play clock is set to 25 or 40 seconds.
explanation: B55 is a defensive player whose helmet comes off, so the play clock is set to 40 seconds. (For an offensive player it would now be 25 seconds under 3-3-10-b-1.)

tags: blocked kick; field goal; illegal touching; out of bounds;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and eight at the B-15. Team A snaps the ball to attempt a field goal. The kick is blocked, crosses the neutral zone and lands at the B-12. Before any player touches it beyond the neutral zone, the ball rebounds behind the neutral zone and goes out of bounds at the B-17. A44 is the last to touch the ball at the B-16 before it goes out of bounds.
choice_a: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-17.
choice_b: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20.
choice_c: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-12.
choice_d: Team A’s ball, fourth and 10 at the B-17.
explanation: A blocked field-goal try that crosses the neutral zone and rebounds behind it, last touched by A before going out of bounds behind the NZ, belongs to Team B at the out-of-bounds spot (B-17). A's touching behind the NZ is not illegal touching.

question_text: Second and 20 at the A-25. During the play, the ball carrier is tackled at the line of scrimmage and fumbles. Several players dive for the ball. B55 grabs A33 and slings him off the pile.
choice_b: Second and five for Team A at the A-40.
choice_c: Both a and b a9-2-5-bre correct answers.
choice_d: Unsportsmanlike conduct foul against B55; automatic first down.
explanation: Slinging an opponent off the pile in a scramble for a loose ball is an unsportsmanlike-conduct foul on B55 carrying an automatic first down for Team A. (Both the personal-foul character and the automatic first down are captured in choice d.)

tags: sideline interference; unsportsmanlike conduct; enforcement spot;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: On the opening kickoff, B22 catches the kick at his goal line and returns the ball down the sideline on Team B’s side of the field. As he moves down the sideline officiating the play, the side judge runs into a Team B coach or squad member in the restricted area (“the white”). B22 is driven out of bounds at the A- 20.
choice_a: Team B is issued an official Sideline Warning.
choice_b: Team B is penalized five yards for the first offense of game administration interference.
choice_c: Team B is penalized 15 yards from the succeeding spot for unsportsmanlike conduct.
choice_d: The foul for this action is treated as a live-ball foul.
explanation: Nonplayer interference in the restricted area that impedes a covering official during a live play is a 15-yard nonplayer foul enforced from the succeeding spot.

tags: taunting; unsportsmanlike conduct; touchdown; enforcement spot;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: During the third quarter, B44 intercepts a pass at the B-40 and returns it for a touchdown. At the A-15, B44 turns and taunts his opponent who is trailing him.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B. The penalty for the taunting foul is enforced on the try or kickoff.
choice_b: No score. 15-yard penalty enforced at the spot of the foul. First and 10 for Team B at the A-30. 3
choice_c: No score. 15-yard penalty from the goal line. First and 10 for Team B at the A-15.
explanation: Taunting an opponent during the run BEFORE reaching the goal line is a live-ball unsportsmanlike foul; the score is cancelled and the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul (A-15), giving Team B first-and-10 at A-30.

tags: unsportsmanlike conduct; taunting; extra period; overtime;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: First possession series in an extra period. B33 intercepts a pass and is running uncontested toward the end zone. Before crossing the goal line B33 points at the opponents bench while high-stepping down the field.
choice_a: The score counts. Team B is penalized on the try.
choice_b: The score counts and the game is over. The penalty is not enforced.
choice_c: The score does not count. The penalty for unsportsmanlike conduct is enforced on the next possession series.
choice_d: The score does not count. The penalty for unsportsmanlike conduct is declined by rule.
explanation: In an extra period, after the change of possession the interception created, B's live-ball unsportsmanlike foul cancels his score (3-1-3-g-2), and unsportsmanlike penalties are among those still enforced on the succeeding possession series.

question_text: After a safety is scored, other than on the try or in an extra period, the ball belongs to the defending team at its own 20-yard line. The ball is put into play by…….
choice_a: a scrimmage kick that may be a punt.
choice_b: a free kick that must be a place kick.
choice_c: a free kick that must be a place kick or a punt.
choice_d: a free kick that may be a punt, drop kick or place kick.
explanation: After a safety the ball is put in play by a free kick from the 20; that free kick may be a punt, drop kick or place kick (no tee required).

question_text: The scoring value for a touchdown:
choice_a: Is always six points. No exceptions.
choice_b: May be either six points or two points, depending on the circumstances.
explanation: A touchdown is six points, except that a defensive/return score on a try counts two points; therefore the value may be six or two depending on the circumstances.

question_text: A team has a total of twelve points. They may reach this total number of points by which combination(s) of scoring plays?
choice_b: One touchdown and two field goals.
choice_c: One touchdown, two safeties, and one field goal.
explanation: Only combinations that total exactly 12: two TDs (12) and one TD + two FGs (6+6=12).

question_text: Kick returner B22 is in his end zone awaiting a punt. He muffs the ball in flight. The ball then hits the ground in the end zone and rolls into the field of play. B22 recovers at the B-2 and returns the ball across Team A’s goal line.
choice_c: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20. 4
choice_d: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-2.
explanation: B22 muffs the punt in his end zone; the muff is not possession, and when he later recovers in the field and runs the length of the field it is a Team B touchdown.

tags: fumble; pylon; goal line; forward progress;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and goal at the B-6 with 1:30 left in the half. Receiver A33 catches a pass in the flat and heads toward the goal line. He is hit at the B-3 and fumbles. The ball rolls forward and strikes the pylon after first touching B55 who attempted to recover the fumble at the B-2.
choice_a: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20.
choice_b: Fourth and goal for Team A at the B-3. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_c: Fourth and goal for Team A at the B-3. Clock starts on the Referee’s signal.
choice_d: Fourth and goal for Team A at the B-2. Clock starts on the snap.
explanation: A33's fumble is touched by B55 in the field and then strikes the pylon; because the last Team A impetus sent it toward/into B's end zone and it went out of bounds at the pylon, it is a touchback (B's ball first-and-10 at the B-20).

tags: out of bounds; illegal touching; covered receiver;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and 10 at the B-16. Receiver A88 voluntarily steps on the sideline at the B-2. He leaps from out of bounds to try to catch the ball. Defender B22 who is covering A88 leaps in front of him and tips the ball; but A88, still airborne, grasps the ball in his hands and first returns to the ground in Team B’s end zone.
choice_a: Foul by A88 for illegal touching of a forward pass.
choice_c: Incomplete pass as A88 is out of bounds when he touches the ball.
choice_d: By stepping out of bounds, A88 is ineligible throughout the down.
explanation: A88 voluntarily stepped out of bounds and is the first to touch the pass while airborne having come from out of bounds; a player out of bounds cannot make a legal catch, so it is an incomplete pass.

tags: out of bounds; illegal touching; covered receiver; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and seven at the A-34 early in the first quarter. In rushing the passer, defensive tackle B77 tips the pass just after the quarterback releases the ball. Tight end A80 steps on the sideline, returns inbounds and is running near the sideline when he catches the pass. He is driven out of bounds at the A-45.
choice_a: Incomplete pass. Fourth and seven at the A-34. Clock on the snap.
choice_b: Foul by A80 for illegal touching of a forward pass. Fourth and seven at the A-34.
choice_c: Legal play. First and 10 for Team A at the A-45. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_d: Legal play. First and 10 for Team A at the A-45. Clock on the Referee’s signal.
explanation: Because the pass was tipped by B77 (a defender), A80's touching after returning inbounds is legal; the catch counts, first-and-10 at A-45 with the clock on the referee's signal after he was driven out of bounds inbounds-established.

question_text: Fourth and 12 at the B-35. The quarterback drops back in the pocket and is still looking for a receiver when B55 is flagged for holding tight end A88 at the B- 30. The quarterback then throws the ball which is muffed by eligible A44 at the B-37 and falls to the ground at the B-30 for an incomplete pass.
choice_b: Fourth and two at the B-25.
explanation: Defensive holding on an incomplete pass is 10 yards from the previous spot and, because it is not defensive pass interference, no automatic first down here: fourth-and-two becomes the ball at the B-25 (still short of the line to gain).

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: Fourth and eight at the B-31. A44 gains five yards and is tackled at the B- 26. After the ball is dead B77 is flagged for a late hit when he dives into an opponent in the pile.
choice_a: Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the B-13.
choice_b: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-13.
choice_c: Team B’s ball, first and 24 at the B-13.
explanation: A44 was stopped short on fourth down, but B77's dead-ball late hit is a 15-yard personal foul with an automatic first down, so Team A keeps the ball first-and-10.

tags: formation; illegal motion; punt; fair catch;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and seven at the A-30. A88 lines up as the widest man on the line of scrimmage. Before the snap he goes in motion (no false start) and is moving laterally behind the offensive linemen when the ball is snapped. The punt travels to the B-25 where B44 makes a fair catch.
choice_a: Fourth and 12 for Team A at the A-25.
choice_b: First and 10 for Team B at the B-30.
choice_c: Either a or b is possible after enforcement of the illegal motion penalty.
explanation: A88 in legal motion is fine, but if there is an illegal-motion/formation issue Team B can choose enforcement from the previous spot or take the ball after the fair catch; both outcomes (either c) are possible depending on B's option.

question_text: The score at halftime is 63-0. Both head coaches approach the Referee about possible clock adjustments for the second half to shorten the game.
choice_a: The third and fourth quarters may be shortened by mutual agreement among the head coaches and the Referee.
choice_b: The second half may be played with a “running clock”—i.e., the rules for stopping the clock may be suspended.
choice_c: Both a and b are legal by rule.
choice_d: The third and fourth quarters may not be shortened and must each be 15 minutes in length.
explanation: Period length may be shortened by mutual agreement of the head coaches and the referee (here for the 3rd and 4th quarters). A 'running clock' that suspends the clock-stopping rules is not permitted by rule.

tags: fumble; out of bounds; forward progress; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second quarter. Safety B22 intercepts a pass and fumbles on the return. A46 recovers the ball at the B-45 and runs to the B-40 where he fumbles. The ball rolls out of bounds at the B-35.
choice_a: First and 10 for Team A at the B-35. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_b: First and 10 for Team A at the B-40. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_c: First and 10 for Team A at the B-40. Clock starts on the Referee's signal.
choice_d: First and 10 for Team A at the B-35. Clock starts on the Referee's signal.
explanation: A46's fumble that goes out of bounds is returned to the spot of the fumble (B-40), not the out-of-bounds spot; Team A first-and-10 at B-40 with the clock starting on the referee's signal (outside the final two minutes).

question_text: During the first quarter B44 intercepts a pass and returns the ball across Team A’s goal line. Covering the play along Team B’s sideline, the Line Judge drops his flag at the A-20 because he has to run around Team B personnel who 6 have come onto the field. Team B has had no game administration warnings to this point in the game.
choice_a: No score. Team B’s ball after enforcement of the penalty for the live- ball foul.
choice_b: No foul. The score counts. Team B is issued its first sideline warning.
choice_c: The score counts. The 15-yard penalty is enforced on the try or the succeeding kickoff.
choice_d: The score counts. The five-yard penalty is enforced on the try or the succeeding kickoff.
explanation: Team B personnel coming onto the field and forcing the covering official to detour is a 15-yard nonplayer unsportsmanlike foul; the score counts and the penalty is enforced on the try or succeeding kickoff.

tags: field goal; fair catch signal; illegal touching;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and eight at the B-34. The field goal attempt is short and the ball is rolling along the ground. Standing at the B-5, B22 gives a fair catch signal as the ball is rolling toward the goal line. B22 does not touch the ball, which then rolls dead at the B-2.
choice_a: First and 10 for Team B at the B-1.
choice_b: First and 10 for Team B at the B-34.
choice_c: First and 10 for Team B at the B-19.
choice_d: First and 10 for Team B at the B-2.
explanation: A fair-catch signal on a kick already rolling on the ground is meaningless (no valid signal, no foul when the ball is untouched). The unsuccessful field goal beyond the 20 belongs to Team B at the previous spot, B-34.

question_text: Defensive back B41 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone and attempts to run it out. While still in the end zone, B41 fumbles the ball which rolls into the field of play. At the B-3, players from both teams attempt to recover the ball, forcing it back into the end zone. The ball goes over the end line.
choice_a: Safety. Two points for Team A.
choice_b: Momentum rule applies. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-3.
choice_c: Touchback. First and 10 for Team B at the B-20.
explanation: B41 intercepts IN his own end zone (not in the field), so the momentum exception does not apply; when the ball is forced back over the end line with Team B responsible for the impetus, it is a safety.

tags: untimed down; dead ball; enforcement spot;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A snaps the ball with five seconds remaining in the first half. End A88 catches a forward pass and is tackled in the field of play. Time in the half expires during the down. After the down is over, B77 is flagged for a late hit.
choice_a: The half is over. The 15-yard penalty is enforced on the second- half kickoff.
choice_b: Automatic first down for Team A after enforcement of the 15-yard penalty. The quarter is extended for one untimed down.
choice_c: The half is over. The penalty is declined by rule.
explanation: Time expired during the down and the foul (B77's late hit) is a dead-ball foul; a dead-ball foul does not extend a period, so the half is over and the 15 yards are enforced on the second-half kickoff.

tags: neutral zone infraction; interception; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and goal at the B-8 late in the fourth quarter. Team A trails by four points. Linebacker B55 intercepts a forward pass in the end zone and returns the ball to the B-12 where he is driven out of bounds. At the end of the down the game clock read 0:06. End B99 was lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. 7
choice_a: Second and goal at the B-4. Game clock starts on the snap.
choice_b: Second and goal at the B-4. Game clock starts on the Referee’s signal.
explanation: B99's offside (no contact) is a live-ball foul declined by rule if Team A prefers the result, but here Team A takes the penalty; second-and-goal at the B-4 with the clock starting on the referee's signal after the 5-yard foul.

tags: replay; replay review; out of bounds; punt;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and five at the A-45. Team A’s punt goes out of bounds at the B-12, but the officials spot the ball at the B-7. The Instant Replay official may interrupt the game to review the out-of-bounds spot.

tags: loose helmet; injured player; 10-second runoff; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Late in the first half, ball carrier A22 is tackled inbounds and as he hits the ground his helmet pops off. Tackle B77 is on the ground with an injury. Team A has no timeouts remaining and Team B has one. When the down is over the game clock reads 0:45.
choice_a: A22 must leave the down for one play.
choice_b: B77 may not remain in the game for the next play.
choice_c: There is no option for a 10-second runoff.
choice_d: All of the above are true.
explanation: A22 (ball carrier, offense) loses his helmet - ball dead, he leaves one down; B77 (defense) is injured and must sit a down; and because affected players are on OPPOSING teams there is no 10-second-runoff option. All statements are true.

question_text: Second and eight at the A-20. B89 sacks quarterback A12 for a 10-yard loss at the A-10. B89 then leaps to his feet and stands over A12, screaming obscenities and shaking his fist in his face. The Referee drops his flag and charges B89 with unsportsmanlike conduct.
choice_a: First and 10 for Team A at the A-35.
choice_b: Third and three for Team A at the A-25.
choice_c: First and 10 for Team A at the A-25.
explanation: B89's screaming/fist-shaking over the downed QB is an unsportsmanlike foul enforced from the succeeding spot (the A-10 dead-ball spot): 15 yards gives Team A first-and-10 at the A-25.

tags: illegal shift; false start; snap; substitution;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A sends in several substitutes after the ball is dead, the players slowly jogging toward their positions. Back A44 continues to jog across the offensive backfield without stopping. The other 10 players stop at their positions but A44 continues his motion when the ball is snapped.
choice_a: The officials shut the action down at the snap and flag Team A for a dead-ball foul—illegal shift that converts to a false start.
choice_b: Live-ball foul at the snap: illegal shift.
choice_c: Legal play since only one player is in motion when the ball is snapped.
explanation: A44 never stops after the shift and is still in continuous backfield motion at the snap while 10 teammates are set; officials shut it down at the snap and flag Team A for the shift/false-start dead-ball foul.

tags: fumble; out of bounds; recovery; touchdown;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Ball carrier A22 is near the sideline at the B-38 when B55 knocks the ball to the ground for a fumble. A22 stumbles out of bounds, returns inbounds, recovers the ball at the B-29 and carries it across Team B’s goal line.
choice_a: The ball is dead when A22 recovers at the B-29.
choice_b: Foul by A22 for returning from out of bounds. 8
explanation: A22 was blocked/knocked out of bounds by the opponent's action but the ball was knocked loose (a fumble, not his voluntary exit); he re-establishes inbounds legally, recovers, and scores - touchdown.

question_text: Second and seven at the A-35. Quarterback A12 is in a shotgun formation. He muffs the backward pass from the snapper. Guard A66 recovers the ball, scrambles and runs outside the tackle box. Unable to find an eligible receiver, A66 at the A-30 throws the ball out of bounds so that it lands beyond the neutral zone extended.
choice_a: Foul for intentional grounding. Team A’s ball, third and 12 at the A-30.
choice_b: Foul for intentional grounding. Team A’s ball, third and 17 at the A-25.
choice_c: Legal play: Team A’s ball, third and seven at the A-35.
explanation: A66 recovered the muffed snap and, outside the tackle box, threw the ball past the neutral zone extended - the legal 'throw-away' exception applies, so it is a legal play, third-and-seven at A-35 (loss of down does not apply).

tags: out of bounds; illegal touching; covered receiver;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Receiver A88 is running a pass route near the sideline. As a pass comes to him he leaps to try to catch it, tips the ball which goes high into the air, and comes to the ground with one foot touching the sideline. He then gets both feet in bounds, grasps the ball with both hands and carries it across Team B’s goal line.
choice_c: Foul for illegal touching of a forward pass. Five yard penalty plus loss of down at the previous spot.
choice_d: Foul for illegal touching of a forward pass. Loss of down at the previous spot.
explanation: A88 touched the sideline (out of bounds) and is the first to touch his own team's forward pass after returning inbounds: illegal touching by an out-of-bounds player is loss of down at the previous spot (no yardage; the '5 yards + loss of down' choice is the ineligible-touch penalty under 7-3-11).

tags: kickoff; blocking below the waist; free kick; enforcement spot;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A kicks off at the A-35. During the kick B33 blocks below the waist at the B-40. Kick returner B38 recovers the bouncing ball at the B-30 and returns to the B-35 where he is driven out of bounds. Team A accepts the penalty.
choice_a: Penalty enforced at the spot of the foul. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-25.
choice_b: 15-yard penalty enforced at the previous spot. Re-kick at the 50- yard line.
choice_c: Penalty enforced at the spot where the kick ends. First and 10 at the B-15.
choice_d: If the block by B33 is at the front in the 10-to-2 range, there is no foul.
explanation: Blocking below the waist during any free kick is illegal for any player except against the ball carrier (9-1-6-c); B33's block is a 15-yard foul enforced from the previous spot, re-kick at the 50.

tags: onside kick; illegal touching; replay review; blocking;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A attempts an on-side kick at the A-35. The ball is untouched by either team at the A-43 when A55 blocks B33 at the waist in the front at the A-47. At the A-43, A11 muffs the ball and B88 recovers the ball while grounded at the A-44.
choice_a: The block by A55 is reviewable, but the Instant Replay official will not initiate a review.
choice_b: A11 is guilty of illegal touching.
choice_c: Both a and b are true. 9
choice_d: If the block by A55 is at the front and above the waist it is legal since it takes place beyond the 10-yard distance.
explanation: On the onside kick A11's touch before the ball travels 10 yards/before Team A is eligible is illegal touching, and the early kicking-team block is reviewable (12-3-4-a, -e) though replay would not create it here.

tags: kickoff; out of bounds; free kick;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A kicks off at the A-35. The kick is untouched at the B-25, where kick returner B33 leaps from inbounds, grasps the ball in both hands, and lands out of bounds.
choice_a: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-25.
choice_b: Foul against Team A for free kick out of bounds. The penalty must be enforced at the previous spot.
choice_c: Foul against Team A for free kick out of bounds. Team B has several penalty options.
explanation: A kick untouched by B that a player carries from inbounds to out of bounds is a kick out of bounds untouched by the receiving team; Team B may take the ball at the 25, at the out-of-bounds spot, or make Team A re-kick after a 5-yard penalty (several options).

tags: blocked kick; safety; impetus; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and 10 at the A-5. The score is tied very late in the fourth quarter. Team A’s punt is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. The punter A80 recovers the ball in the end zone and completes a forward pass to eligible receiver A44 at the A-40. A44 carries the ball to the B-5 where he is tackled in bounds. When the clock is stopped it reads 0:06.
choice_a: The ball is dead when A80 recovers on the end zone: Touchback.
choice_b: The ball is dead when A80 recovers on the end zone: Safety
choice_c: Team A’s ball, first and goal at the B-5. Game clock starts on the Referee’s signal.
choice_d: Team A’s ball, first and goal at the B-5. Game clock starts on the snap.
explanation: A blocked punt that does not cross the neutral zone remains a live ball that Team A may recover and advance; A80 recovers in the end zone and completes a legal pass - Team A first-and-goal at the B-5, clock on the snap.

tags: fair catch signal; punt; PSK; touchback; blocking below the waist;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Punt receiver B44 signals for a fair catch at the B-35 and lets the ball go over his head without touching it. At the B-30 he blocks gunner A88 in the front at the waist. The ball bounces into the end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: The result of the play is a touchback.
choice_b: The block by B44 is illegal.
choice_c: Assuming that B44's block is illegal, the penalty is governed by postscrimmage kick rules.
choice_d: All of the above answers are true.
explanation: The untouched kick over the end line is a touchback; B44's block after signaling a fair catch is an illegal block; and because B is the receiving team fouling during a scrimmage kick that crossed the NZ, post-scrimmage-kick enforcement governs. All statements are true.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
tags: extra period; overtime; blocked kick; field goal;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: In the first possession series of an extra period, Team A attempts a field goal. The kick is blocked and the ball does not cross the neutral zone. A44 recovers and carries the ball beyond the line to gain before he is tackled.
choice_a: Team A’s possession series continues, first and 10 at the dead- ball spot.
choice_b: Team A’s possession series is over and Team B will start its possession series at the original 25-yard line.
explanation: In an extra period a field goal blocked behind the neutral zone is still Team A's ball; A44 recovers and advances beyond the line to gain for a first down, so Team A's possession series continues.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
tags: extra period; overtime; blocked kick; field goal;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: In the first possession series of an extra period, Team A attempts a field goal. The kick is blocked and the ball crosses the neutral zone. The ball hits B55 in the leg and A33 recovers while grounded.
choice_a: Team A’s possession series continues, first and 10 at the dead- ball spot.
choice_b: Team A’s possession series is over and Team B will start its possession series at the original 25-yard line.
explanation: The blocked kick crosses the NZ and is touched by B; Team A recovers and, having reached beyond the line to gain, earns a first down, so its possession series continues. (Team A may not ADVANCE a kick recovered beyond the NZ, but a recovery beyond the line to gain still produces a new series.)

question_text: Late in the fourth quarter Team A trails by four points and has used all its timeouts. Ball carrier A44 is running near the sideline. As he is about to be tackled in bounds, he throws the ball backward and out of bounds to stop the clock. The line judge drops a flag for an illegal backward pass out of bounds to conserve time. When the ball becomes dead A88 is on the ground with an injury. The game clock reads 0:55.
choice_a: A88 is not required to leave the game if medical personnel determine that he may continue to play.
choice_b: Team B may elect a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: Play clock is set to 40 seconds.
choice_d: If there is a 10-second runoff the clock starts on the snap.
explanation: A44's illegal backward pass out of bounds to stop the clock is a 10-second-runoff foul; the injured player A88 is Team A (same team as the fouling side), so the opponent Team B may elect the 10-second runoff.

question_text: Late in the fourth quarter Team A trails by four points and has used all its timeouts. Ball carrier A44 is running near the sideline. As he is about to be tackled in bounds, he throws the ball backward and out of bounds to stop the clock. The line judge drops a flag for an illegal backward pass out of bounds to conserve time. When the ball becomes dead B63 is on the ground with an injury. The game clock reads 0:55.
choice_a: B63 is not required to leave the game if medical personnel determine that he may continue to play.
choice_b: Team B may elect a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: Play clock is set to 40 seconds.
choice_d: If there is a 10-second runoff the clock starts on the snap.
explanation: Here the injured player B63 is a DEFENSIVE player, so following the injury the play clock is set to 40 seconds (an offensive-player injury would set 25).

question_text: Late in the fourth quarter Team A trails by four points and has used all its timeouts. During a play in which A33’s helmet comes off, A79 goes down with an injury. Ball carrier A48 is tackled in bounds short of the line to gain. The game clock reads 0:55.
choice_a: A79 is not required to leave the game if medical personnel determine that he may continue to play.
choice_b: Team B may elect a 10-second runoff.
choice_c: Play clock is set to 40 seconds.
choice_d: If there is a 10-second runoff the clock starts on the snap.
explanation: A33's helmet and A79's injury both involve Team A players (same team), so the opponent Team B may elect the 10-second runoff (the no-runoff bar applies only to opposing players).

tags: ineligible touching; intentional grounding; forward pass;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and seven at the A-23. A12 takes the snap and drops straight back. He scrambles outside the tackle box and, under pressure at the A-16, throws the ball away. The pass hits the ground near the hash mark at the A-27. Tackle A77 was at the A-30 when A12 released the pass.
choice_b: Foul by A77, ineligible receiver downfield.
choice_c: Both a and b are correct answers.
explanation: The QB scrambled outside the tackle box and threw past the neutral zone (legal throw-away, no grounding), but tackle A77 was three-plus yards downfield when the pass was thrown - foul, ineligible receiver downfield.

tags: ineligible touching; enforcement spot; forward pass;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and seven at the A-23. A12 takes the snap and drops straight back. He scrambles outside the tackle box and, under pressure at the A-16, throws the ball away. The pass hits the ground near the hash mark at the A-27. Tackle A77 was at the A-30 when A12 released the pass. What is the correct down and distance?
choice_b: Third and seven at the A-23.
explanation: Ineligible receiver downfield is 5 yards from the previous spot with the down repeated: from second-and-seven at the A-23, the ball goes back to the A-18 and it is second-and-12.

question_text: Second and 2 at the A-15. B44 intercepts a forward pass at the A-25 and returns to the A-10 where he fumbles. The ball rolls to the A-8 where A22 bats it backward. The ball strikes the goal line pylon.
choice_a: Touchback. Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the A-20.
choice_b: The rules apply for a forward fumble going out of bounds.
choice_c: Safety. Two points for Team B.
choice_d: Foul by A22, illegally batting the ball.
explanation: After B44's interception and fumble, A22 bats the loose ball backward and it strikes the pylon; Team A supplied the impetus/illegal bat sending it out of bounds behind the goal line - a safety, two points for Team B.

question_text: A88 receives a forward pass at the B-20 and races down the sideline. At the B-10 he is hit; the ball pops out of his hands and goes high into the air. B22 intercepts the fumble at the B-7 and his momentum takes him into his end zone. In attempting to get out of the end zone B22 fumbles the ball forward into the field of play. The ball bounces out of bounds at the B-2.
choice_a: Momentum rule applies. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-7.
choice_b: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-2.
choice_c: Safety. Two points for Team A.
choice_d: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the A-20.
explanation: B22 recovered the fumble at the B-7 (outside the momentum zone), so the momentum exception does not apply; his forward fumble from the end zone returns to the spot of the fumble in the end zone - a safety for Team A.

question_text: Team A kicks off at its 35-yard line. As the ball rolls along the ground at the B-3, B11 kicks it backward into his end zone. The ball bounces over the end line.
choice_a: Foul by B11, illegally kicking the ball.
choice_b: The result of the play is a safety - two points for Team A.
choice_c: If the penalty is accepted the down is repeated with the ball re-kicked at the A-45.
explanation: B11 illegally kicks the free-kick ball (a foul); with B supplying the impetus over its own end line the play is a safety; and if the penalty is accepted the free kick is repeated after the 10-yard penalty (re-kick at A-45). All statements are true.

question_text: Kick returner B88 catches a punt in his end zone. In his attempt to run it out, he is hit and fumbles. Teammate B44 recovers and also attempts to run the ball out of the end zone. He makes it to the goal line and his feet are in the field of play, but the ball in his arms has not broken the goal-line plane. B44 fumbles the ball which in the ensuing scramble is forced over the end line.
choice_a: Safety. Two points for Team A.
choice_b: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20.
explanation: B44 never broke the plane of the goal line with the ball, so he had not left the end zone; his fumble that goes over the end line is a touchback (the ball's forced position, not his feet, controls).

tags: illegal use of hands; roughing the passer; enforcement spot;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and eight at the A-30. Quarterback A12 drops back to pass. Defensive end B99 is flagged for a head-slap to tackle A77. Receiver A88 catches the pass and is tackled at the A-40.
choice_d: B99 is also charged with unsportsmanlike conduct.
explanation: B99's head-slap is a 15-yard personal foul enforced from the end of the completed pass run (B-40): first-and-10 at the B-45. A head-slap is not automatically unsportsmanlike.

question_text: Third and 15 at the B-25, very near the end of the fourth quarter. Team A trails by 25-20. Quarterback A12 runs to the B-23 and completes a pass to A88 in the end zone, and the Line Judge drops his flag for the foul. The game clock goes to 0:00 during the down.
choice_a: The penalty is accepted. The score does not count, the period is not extended, and Team B wins 25-20.
choice_b: Extend the period. Fourth and 20 at the B-30.
choice_c: Extend the period. Fourth and 18 at the B-28.
explanation: Time expired during the down and Team A committed the foul; a period is not extended for the offense's own live-ball foul, so the score does not count, the period ends, and Team B wins.

tags: unsportsmanlike conduct; penalty on the try; untimed down;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Receiver A44 scores a touchdown on a long pass play as the game clock runs down to 0:00 in the first half. In celebration, A44 runs to the stands and leaps up to exchange high-fives with fans. The Back Judge correctly flags him for unsportsmanlike conduct.
choice_a: The penalty must be enforced on the try—no option.
choice_b: The period is extended for the try. If Team B chooses, the period may be extended further and the penalty enforced on the kickoff before halftime.
choice_c: The period is extended for the try. Team B may choose to have the penalty enforced on the try or on the second-half kickoff.
explanation: The scoring play with time expired extends the period for the try; A44's dead-ball unsportsmanlike foul may, at Team B's option, be enforced on the try or on the second-half kickoff.

tags: unsportsmanlike conduct; penalty on the try; blocked kick; extra period;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A scores a touchdown as time expires in the fourth quarter, tying the score at 48-48. B77 blocks the kick attempt on the try, and B33 recovers the ball. As he races for Team A’s end zone, at the 10-yard line B33 turns around and moon-walks across the goal line with his fist pumping in the air. The Referee drops his flag for unsportsmanlike conduct.
choice_a: The penalty is declined by rule. Team B wins 50-48.
choice_b: No score by B33. The score remains tied, and the penalty is enforced at the beginning of the first extra period.
choice_c: No score by B33. The penalty is declined by rule and the game moves to the extra period.
explanation: On the try, B33's unsportsmanlike act before crossing the goal line cancels his two-point return; the score stays tied and the 15-yard penalty carries to the start of the first extra period.

tags: neutral zone infraction; unsportsmanlike conduct; disqualification; offsetting;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and seven at the B-30. Defensive tackle B77 is in the neutral zone (no contact) at the snap. Ball carrier A22 breaks free and is at the B-5 when he begins a “goose-step” into the end zone, drawing a flag from the officials. This is the second time in the game that A22 has been charged with unsportsmanlike conduct.
choice_a: The offside penalty is declined by rule and the touchdown counts. A22 is disqualified.
choice_b: The fouls offset and the down is repeated. A22 is disqualified.
choice_c: Both penalties are enforced in the order of their occurrence. A22 is disqualified.
choice_d: A22 is allowed to remain in the game since the fouls offset.
explanation: B's neutral-zone foul (no contact) and A22's unsportsmanlike foul are fouls by both teams during the down: they offset and the down is repeated; A22's second unsportsmanlike foul of the game disqualifies him regardless.

question_text: Second and seven at the B-10. The game clock is running late in the fourth quarter. At the snap Team A has five players in the backfield. When ball carrier A22 is tackled at the B-5 the game clock shows 25 seconds.
choice_a: Team B may decline the 10-second runoff and accept the penalty.
choice_b: Team B must accept the penalty in order to have the 10-second runoff.
choice_c: The 10-second runoff rule does not apply to this situation.
explanation: Illegal formation (five in the backfield) does not cause the clock to stop immediately, so it is not a 10-second-runoff foul; the runoff rule simply does not apply here (3-4-4-b).

question_text: Third and five at the A-40 early in the first quarter. Quarterback A12 drops back to pass. Under a heavy rush, at the A-30 he throws the ball forward and it falls to the ground at the feet of back A44. A44 is pass-protection blocking at the A-35 and is not aware that the pass has been thrown.
choice_a: Foul, intentional grounding since A44 has no opportunity to catch the pass.
choice_b: If the Referee rules intentional grounding the penalty is five yards at the spot of the foul plus loss of down. 14
choice_c: The game clock starts on the referee’s signal.
explanation: The QB, inside the tackle box under rush, throws to the ground at the feet of A44 who is pass-blocking and has no realistic chance to catch it - intentional grounding, 5 yards from the spot of the foul plus loss of down.

question_text: B47 intercepts a pass at the B-3. His momentum carries him into the end zone where he fumbles the ball. It rolls into the field of play and out of bounds at the B-8.
choice_a: The momentum exception applies. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-3.
choice_b: The momentum exception does not apply. Safety. Two points for Team A.
choice_c: The momentum exception does not apply. Team B’s ball at the B-8.
explanation: B47 intercepts at the B-3 (inside the five) and momentum carries him into the end zone where he fumbles out of bounds in the field; the momentum exception applies - Team B's ball first-and-10 at the B-3, not a safety.

tags: punt; illegal touching; clipping; penalty on the try;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: After A23 is the first to touch a punt beyond the neutral zone at the B-25, B45 recovers at the B-30. B45 fumbles at the B-35, and while the ball is loose B80 clips at the B-40. A39 recovers and carries the ball across Team B’s goal line.
choice_a: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-25.
choice_b: Touchdown counts. The clipping penalty is enforced on the try or the kickoff.
choice_c: Foul by A23 for illegal touching.
explanation: A23's first touching only gives B the right to take the ball at that spot; here the return produces a Team A touchdown, so the touchdown counts and B80's clip is enforced on the try or succeeding kickoff.

question_text: Team A punts on fourth and seven at the B-45. B63 clips at the B-32 while the punt is in the air. Punt receiver B21 catches the kick in his own end zone and retreats across the end line.
choice_a: Postscrimmage kick enforcement. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B- 17.
choice_b: Postscrimmage kick enforcement. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-10.
choice_c: Safety. Two points for Team A.
choice_d: 15-yard penalty at the previous spot. First and 10 for Team A.
explanation: B's clip during the scrimmage kick is post-scrimmage-kick enforced from the PSK spot; a kick ending in the end zone makes the PSK spot the 20, and the 15-yard penalty is capped at half the distance, giving Team B first-and-10 at the B-10.

question_text: Third and seven at the B-30. The quarterback sprints to his right outside the tackle box. Under a heavy rush, he reverses directions until he is directly behind the location of the ball at the snap. At this point on the B-36 he throws a forward pass that hits the ground at the B-25. There are no eligible Team A receivers in the area.
choice_a: Legal play. Fourth and seven at the B-30.
choice_b: Intentional grounding. Fourth and 13 at the B-36.
choice_c: Intentional grounding. Fourth and 18 at the B-41.
explanation: The QB scrambled but then threw from a point directly behind where the ball was snapped (i.e., back within the tackle-box area) with no eligible receiver near - the throw-away exception does not apply; legal only because the ball reached the line/area - here ruled a legal play, fourth-and-seven at the B-30.

question_text: Fourth and 10 at the A-5. Punter A27 is standing in his end zone when he punts. The line-drive kick crosses the neutral zone and strikes B38 in the helmet at the A-12. The ball then rebounds back into Team A’s end zone, where A27 recovers it and carries it to the A-10.
choice_a: Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the A-10. Clock starts on the ready.
choice_b: Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the A-10. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_c: Ball is dead when A27 recovers. Safety; two points for Team B.
choice_d: Ball is dead when A27 recovers. Touchback; Team A’s ball at the A-20.
explanation: The punt from the end zone strikes B38 beyond the NZ and rebounds into A's end zone where A27 recovers; the kick (Team A) supplied the impetus into the end zone, so A27's recovery there is a safety, two points for Team B.

tags: neutral zone infraction; interception; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and four at the A-26. B79 is flagged for being in the neutral zone at the snap but he does not make contact with an opponent prior to the snap. B35 intercepts A12’s forward pass and is driven out of bounds at the A-48.
choice_a: 5-yard penalty. First and 10 for Team A at the A-31. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_b: 5-yard penalty. First and 10 for Team A at the A-31. Clock starts on the ready.
choice_c: Dead-ball foul against B79. Officials stop the action when B79 enters the neutral zone.
explanation: B79's offside without contact is a live-ball foul; Team A declines the interception result and takes the 5 yards: first-and-10 at the A-31 with the clock on the ready-for-play signal.

tags: illegal touching; forward pass; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and 10 at the 50-yard line. A17’s legal forward pass ricochets off A65’s helmet before touching any other player or an official. Eligible receiver A27 then catches the ball and is driven out of bounds at the B-38. The game clock shows 1:35 in the second quarter.
choice_a: Legal play. First and 10 at the B-38. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_b: Foul by A65, illegal touching. Five-yard penalty, previous spot.
choice_c: Foul by A65, Illegal touching. Five-yard penalty, previous spot plus loss of down.
choice_d: Legal play. First and 10 at the B-38. Clock starts on the ready.
explanation: A pass that ricochets off A65's own helmet is not 'touching' that makes an ineligible illegally touch; A27's subsequent catch is legal - first-and-10 at the B-38, clock on the snap.

tags: punt; fair catch; illegal touching; blocking;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A punts on fourth and seven at the A-40. Receiver B27 is at the B-30 where he signals for a fair catch after the ball has hit the ground. A20 then blocks B44 above the waist, causing B44 to touch the ball. A78 recovers the ball while grounded at the B-32.
choice_a: Foul by B27 for an illegal fair catch signal.
choice_b: Illegal touching by A78. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-32.
choice_c: Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the B-32.
explanation: After the kick hits the ground, A20's legal block drives B44 into the ball (forced touching, not a foul by B); A78 then recovers - because B's touch was forced, Team A keeps the ball first-and-10 at the B-32.

question_text: Second quarter. Third and eight at the A-35. Ball carrier A24 is driven out of bounds at the A-45. During the run A78 clips at the A-40. When the ball is dead the game clock reads 2:00. 16
choice_a: 15-yard penalty at the previous spot. Clock starts on the ready.
choice_b: 15-yard penalty at the spot of the foul. Clock starts on the ready.
choice_c: 15-yard penalty at the spot of the foul. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_d: 15-yard penalty at the previous spot. Clock starts on the snap.
explanation: A78's clip during A24's run is enforced from the previous spot (the basic spot for a foul by the offense behind the end of the run); 15 yards from the A-35, and the clock starts on the snap after the runner went out of bounds.

tags: field goal; illegal touching; touchback; punt;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and eight at Team B’s 40-yard line. Team A attempts a field goal. B38 touches the ball at Team B’s 10-yard line, and it then rolls into the end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-40.
choice_b: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-10.
choice_c: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20.
choice_d: Safety—two points for Team A. Team B free kicks at the B-20.
explanation: On the field-goal attempt, B38 touches the ball at the B-10 and it rolls over the end line; the touch by B does not change possession and the ball ending in the end zone gives Team B first-and-10 at the B-20 (touchback).

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: On the first possession series of an extra period B37 intercepts a legal forward pass at the B-5 and returns the ball to the B-25, where he is tackled. During the run B78 holds in his end zone.
choice_a: Safety by penalty. Two points for Team A. Team A’s possession series is over. Team B starts its possession series at the original 25-yard line.
choice_b: The penalty is declined by rule. Team B starts its possession series at the original 25-yard line.
choice_c: Team A’s possession series is over. The penalty carries over to Team B’s possession series. Team B will put the ball in play at the original 35-yard line.
explanation: After B's interception (a change of possession) in the extra period, B78's holding in the end zone would be a safety, but penalties after a change of possession are declined by rule in extra periods; Team B simply starts its series at the 25.

tags: momentum; backward pass; safety; extra period;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: B29 intercepts a legal forward pass at the B-1 and fumbles at the B-4. B25 races in and recovers the fumble at the B-3, his momentum carrying him into the end zone. To avoid being tackled, B25 throws a backward pass which teammate B37 muffs. B37 and A83 simultaneously recover the ball in the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown; six points for Team A.
choice_b: Momentum rule: First and 10 for Team B at the B-3.
choice_c: Safety; two points for Team A.
choice_d: Momentum rule: First and 10 for Team B at the B-4.
explanation: B25 recovered at the B-3 with momentum into the end zone, then threw a backward pass muffed and recovered simultaneously in the end zone; with Team B responsible for the ball being in the end zone and the simultaneous recovery in B's end zone, the result is a safety.

tags: ineligible touching; enforcement spot; screen pass;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and eight at the A-45. Thinking that quarterback A12 is going to throw a screen pass, tackle A73 goes downfield to the B-49. He then remembers that the pass is to be thrown deep, so he hustles back to the scrimmage line before the pass is thrown. A12 is at the A-40 when he completes a forward pass to eligible A80 who catches the ball and steps out of bounds at the B-40.
choice_a: Five–yard penalty at the previous spot. Second and 13 at the A- 40.
choice_b: Five–yard penalty at the previous spot plus loss of down. Third and 13 at the A-40. 17
choice_c: Legal play. First and 10 at the B-40.
choice_d: Five-yard penalty at the spot of the foul.
explanation: A73 went downfield beyond three yards before the pass; returning behind the line does not cure it - once he has been illegally downfield it is a foul, 5 yards from the previous spot: second-and-13 at the A-40.

tags: clipping; blocking below the waist; enforcement spot;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and 12 at the A-45. A12 takes the direct snap and drops back into the pocket to pass. At the A-44 guard A67 is in the blocking zone when just after the snap he blocks defensive lineman B70 just above the ankles from the back. A12 completes the pass to eligible receiver A88 who is tackled inbounds at the B-40.
choice_a: Legal play since clipping is allowed inside the blocking zone. First and 10 at the B-40.
choice_b: Foul for clipping. Fifteen-yard penalty at the spot of the foul. Third and 28 at the A-29.
choice_c: Foul for clipping. Fifteen-yard penalty at the previous spot. Third and 27 at the A-30.
explanation: Even inside the blocking zone, a lineman may not block from behind at or below the knee (clipping); A67's block just above the ankles from the back is a foul, 15 yards from the previous spot - third-and-27 at the A-30.

question_text: Second and 10 at the A-40 late in the fourth quarter. Back A30 takes the handoff from the quarterback at the A-38 and runs to the A-45 where he is hit and fumbles. During the fumble, A78 holds B73 at the A-48, and B52 shoves A36 in the back above the waist at the 50 in an attempt to recover the fumble. In the scramble to recover, the ball rolls out of bounds at the B-45. When the ball is dead the game clock reads 1:13.
choice_a: First and 10 for Team A at the B-45.
choice_b: Second and 12 at the A-38 following enforcement of the holding penalty.
choice_c: Second and 15 at the A-35 following enforcement of the holding penalty.
choice_d: Holding by A78 and blocking in the back by B52 are offsetting fouls. Repeat second and 10 at the A-40.
explanation: B52's shove in the back to reach the loose ball is a legal push to recover a fumble (not a foul), so only A78's holding stands; enforced from the previous spot the result is second-and-15 at the A-35.

tags: neutral zone infraction; formation; blocked kick; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and six at the B-26 very late in the 4th quarter. At the snap B66 is in the neutral zone and Team A has five players in the backfield. B66 blocks a field goal attempt and the ball never crosses the line of scrimmage. A22 recovers the ball on the B-29 with 1 sec remaining in the game.
choice_b: Game clock starts on the snap.
explanation: B66 in the neutral zone and Team A's five-in-the-backfield formation are fouls by both teams during the down: they offset and third down is repeated, with the clock starting on the snap. Both statements are true.

tags: roughing the kicker; disqualification; field goal;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and 16 at the B-21. B92 blocks the field goal attempt and then bumps into the kicker, knocking him to the ground. The ball crosses the neutral zone and is bouncing at the B-2 where A76 touches it. B44 picks up the ball at 18 the B-3 and is tackled at the B-5. After the ball is dead kicker A10 curses B92. B92 swings at A10 and misses. In response, A10 slugs B92.
choice_a: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-5. B92 and A10 are disqualified.
choice_b: Running into the kicker. Fourth and 11 at the B-16. A10 is disqualified.
choice_c: Roughing the kicker. First and 10 at the 10.5. B92 and A10 are disqualified.
choice_d: Clock starts on the ready. B92 and A10 are disqualified.
explanation: B92 blocked the kick, so contact with the kicker is not roughing/running; the play stands with the ball at the B-5, and the post-dead-ball fighting between A10 and B92 disqualifies both.

tags: ineligible touching; offensive pass interference; forward pass;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and eight at the A-45. Quarterback A12 drops back to pass. Tackle A76 initiates a block against B94 at the line of scrimmage and drives him downfield to the A-48. B94 drops back into coverage and A76 retreats back behind the line of scrimmage. A12 rolls out of the pocket and completes a pass to end A88 who is tackled at the B-45.
choice_a: Legal play. First and 10 for Team A at the B-45.
choice_c: A76 is an ineligible receiver downfield.
explanation: A76 initiated a legal block at the line and drove the defender downfield, then retreated behind the line before the pass; a legal block that carries a lineman downfield is not ineligible-downfield or OPI - legal play, first-and-10 at the B-45.

tags: intentional grounding; grounding; 10-second runoff; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and goal at the B-3 in the fourth quarter. The game clock is running and reads 0:10 when the ball is snapped. Team A trails by four points and is out of timeouts. Quarterback A12 muffs the snap, catches the muffed ball and immediately throws the ball forward to the ground. The game clock reads 0:06.
choice_a: Foul by A12 for intentional grounding. Clock starts on the referee’s signal.
choice_b: Legal play, fourth and goal at the B-3. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_c: Foul by A12 for intentional grounding. Due to the 10-second runoff, the game is over.
explanation: A12 caught his own muffed snap (so he is a runner/passer who then throws) and immediately threw it to the ground with no receiver - a legal spike is only immediately after a hand-to-hand snap; here it is intentional grounding is avoided because he recovered and threw - ruled legal, fourth-and-goal, clock on the snap.

tags: blocking below the waist; offsetting; facemask; safety;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Third and 10 at the B-30. B79 is lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. B22 intercepts a forward pass in his end zone. As B22 is trying to get out of the end zone, A33 grasps and twists his facemask. B22 then fumbles the ball from the end zone into the field of play. During the fumble B33 blocks A22 below the waist at the B-12. B44 recovers the ball at the B-5.
choice_a: The penalty for B33’s foul results in a safety.
choice_b: The fouls offset—no option. Repeat third and 10 at the B-30.
choice_c: Team B may refuse offsetting fouls. After the penalty for B33’s block below the waist, Team B will have first and 10 at the B-6.
explanation: Fouls by both teams during the down (A's facemask and B's block below the waist after the change of possession) offset and the down is repeated.

question_text: Third and 13 at the B-45 late in the fourth quarter. Leading by four points, Team A throws a forward pass that B44 intercepts. As B44 is about to be tackled 19 at the B-30 he throws the ball forward and is flagged for an illegal forward pass. The pass falls to the ground incomplete. When the ball is dead the game clock reads 0:13.
choice_a: The 10-second runoff does not apply because the foul is after a change of possession.
choice_b: After the yardage penalty and the 10-second runoff Team B will have first and 10 at the B-25. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal.
choice_c: After the yardage penalty and the 10-second runoff Team B will have first and 10 at the B-25. The clock starts on the snap.
explanation: B44's illegal forward pass occurs AFTER a change of possession (his interception), and the 10-second-runoff rule applies only to fouls before a change of team possession - so no runoff here.

tags: kickoff; free kick; formation; illegal formation;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A is in formation to kick off at the A-35. A33 and A44 are both positioned in a four-point stance, with their feet at the A-29 and their hands at the A-31. The untouched kick hits the ground in Team B’s end zone where the officials declare it dead.
choice_b: The accepted penalty for the illegal formation requires that the ball be re-kicked—no option.
choice_c: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-25.
choice_d: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-30.
explanation: Free-kick formation requires the required number of players and proper positioning; the violation here is enforced with the receiving team taking the ball (first-and-10 at the B-30 per the option), not a mandatory re-kick.

tags: onside kick; illegal touching; replay review; blocking;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A’s on-side kick from the A-35 is untouched at the A-43 when A55 blocks B44 above the waist in the front at the A-46. A28 muffs the ball at the A- 44. After the ball rolls to the A-46, A88 blocks B22 above the waist in the front at the A-42. A20 recovers while grounded at the A-44.
choice_b: The block by A55 is a foul.
choice_c: The block by A88 is legal.
explanation: A28's touch before Team A is eligible is illegal touching; A55's early block is a foul; and A88's above-the-waist front block is legal in context - all true.

question_text: B22 is standing at the B-35 in position to catch a punt and teammate B88 is a few yards in front of him at the B-38. Down field to cover the kick, A44 legally blocks B88, driving him into B22 just as the ball reaches him. The ball hits B22 in the shoulder, and A55 recovers at the B-25.
choice_b: Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the B-25.
choice_c: A44 is guilty of kick-catch interference.
choice_d: B22’s touching of the ball is forced touching.
explanation: A44 legally blocks B88 into B22, forcing B22 to touch the ball (forced touching by B, not a foul and not first-touching by A); A55 recovers - Team A's ball first-and-10 at the B-25.

question_text: Second and 12 at the A-4. Quarterback A12 retreats into his end zone to pass. The offensive line drops back into the end zone for pass protection. Unable to find an open receiver down the field, A12 attempts to throw the ball 20 away. The pass goes toward guard A66 who is two yards deep in the end zone. To prevent an interception, A66 reaches out and deliberately muffs the ball, and it falls to the ground. End A88 is standing next to A66 and also tries to catch the pass.
choice_a: Foul by A66, illegal touching of a forward pass. The penalty results in a safety.
choice_b: Foul by A66, illegal touching of a forward pass. Second and 14 at the A-2.
choice_c: Foul by A66, illegal touching of a forward pass. Third and 14 at the A- 2.
explanation: A66 (an ineligible) deliberately touches his own forward pass in the end zone - illegal touching, 5 yards from the previous spot plus loss of down; walked off it is second-and-14 at the A-2, not a safety.

question_text: Third and five at the B-20. B44 intercepts a forward pass at the B-3 and his momentum carries him into his end zone. While still in the end zone B44 fumbles the ball, which rolls forward into the field of play. In the scramble for the ball it goes back into Team B’s end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20.
choice_b: Momentum rule applies. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-3.
choice_c: Safety. Two points for Team A.
explanation: B44 intercepts at the B-3 with momentum into the end zone, then fumbles; the ball is forced back over the end line with Team B responsible for the new impetus - a safety (the momentum exception protects only against a ball becoming dead in B's possession, not this forced-back sequence).

question_text: During a down in the first quarter B55’s helmet comes off without a helmet foul by Team A. B55 immediately puts his helmet back on and chases the ball carrier.
choice_a: B55 must leave the game for the next down but there is no foul.
choice_b: B55 may remain in the game for the next down.
choice_c: Personal foul by B55 but he does not need to leave the game.
choice_d: B55 must leave the game for one down, and he is charged with a personal foul.
explanation: A player whose helmet comes off must leave for the next down; if he keeps playing beyond the immediate action (chasing the runner), that prolonged participation is a personal foul - so B55 both draws a foul and must leave one down.

question_text: Third and eight at the A-35. Ball carrier A24 is tackled inbounds at the A-45. During the run A78 clips at the A-40.
choice_d: The basic spot is the A-40.
explanation: A78's clip during A24's run (a foul by the offense behind the end of the run) is enforced from the previous spot - the basic spot is the A-35, so the 15 yards come off there.

question_text: Team A punts on fourth and seven at the B-45. B63 clips at the B-32 while the punt is in the air. Punt receiver B21 catches the kick in his own end zone and retreats across the end line.
choice_a: The result of the play is a safety.
choice_b: The foul by B63 is covered by postscrimmage kick rules.
choice_c: The penalty is enforced at the B-32. 21
choice_d: The penalty is enforced at the previous spot.
explanation: B63's clip during the scrimmage kick that crossed the NZ is covered by post-scrimmage-kick enforcement (foul by the receiving team who will next put the ball in play).

tags: PSK; fair catch; blocking below the waist; offsetting;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Team A punts on fourth and 12 at the A-25. Team A is flagged for an illegal shift. Receiver B28 signals and makes a fair catch at the B-40. During the kick B72 blocks below the waist at the B-45. Under Exception 2 to Rule 10-1-4, Team B has the initial option, and it refuses offsetting fouls.
choice_a: If Team A accepts the penalty, it will be enforced at the A-25.
choice_b: If Team A accepts the penalty, it will be enforced at the B-45.
choice_c: Team A must accept the penalty--no option.
explanation: With offsetting fouls refused under the Exception to 10-1-4, Team A may accept the penalty for B's block below the waist; enforced by PSK rules the ball is placed accordingly (choice d best reflects the layered options).

tags: loose helmet; play clock; game clock;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Defensive end B99’s helmet comes off as he is making a tackle.
choice_a: The game clock continues to run after the ball is dead.
choice_b: The game clock is stopped immediately when B99's helmet comes off.
choice_c: The play clock is set to 40 seconds.
choice_d: Whether the play clock is set to 40 or 25 depends on whether there is a 10-second runoff.
explanation: B99 is a defensive player, so his helmet coming off sets the play clock to 40 seconds. The game clock stops at the END of the down (not immediately), and the 40/25 choice depends on offense/defense, not on any runoff.

question_text: Team A punts on fourth and 12 at the A-35. B23 signals for a fair catch at the 50-yard line but does not touch the ball. While the ball is rolling on the ground, B80 at the B-45 pushes A82 in the back above the waist in an attempt to get to the ball. B80 recovers the ball while grounded at the B-48.
choice_a: Team B's ball, first and 10 at the B-48.
choice_c: Foul by B23 for giving a fair catch signal and not touching the ball.
explanation: B23's fair-catch signal on a ball he never touches is not itself a foul; B80's push in the back to reach the loose ball is a legal attempt to recover a kick, so Team B keeps the ball first-and-10 at the B-48.

question_text: First and 10 at the B-25. Team A commits an illegal shift on a play in which A34 carries the ball across Team B’s goal line.
choice_a: First and 15 at the B-30. Game clock starts on the snap.
choice_b: First and 15 at the B-30. Game clock starts on the Referee's signal.
explanation: The illegal shift is a live-ball foul that nullifies A34's touchdown; 5 yards from the previous spot makes it first-and-15 at the B-30 with the clock on the snap.

question_text: Fourth and eight at Team B’s 40-yard line. Team A attempts a field goal. B38 touches the ball at Team B’s 10-yard line, and it then rolls into the end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Touchback. First and 10 for Team B at the B-25.
choice_b: Touchback. First and 10 for Team B at the B-20.
choice_c: Not a touchback. First and 10 for Team B at the B-40.
explanation: B38's touch at the B-10 is not first-touching (only Team A can first-touch); the kick then goes over the end line - touchback, Team B first-and-10 at the B-20.

question_text: Third and eight at the B-15. Late in the fourth quarter, Team A trails by four points. The game clock is running when A66 commits a false start, and the officials shut the play down with eight seconds remaining in the game. Team A has no timeouts remaining.
choice_a: Third and 13 at the B-20. Game clock starts on the Referee’s signal.
choice_b: Third and 13 at the B-20. Game clock starts on the snap.
choice_c: The foul qualifies for a 10-second runoff. The game is over.
explanation: A false start with the clock running prevents the snap and qualifies for a 10-second runoff; with eight seconds left and no Team A timeouts, the runoff ends the game.

question_text: Team A punts on fourth and seven at the B-45. B66 holds at the B-46 while the punt is in the air. Punt receiver B21 catches the kick in his own end zone and is tackled before he can get out of the end zone.
choice_a: 10-yard penalty at the previous spot. Team A’s ball, first and 10 at the B-35.
choice_b: The penalty results in a safety. Two points for Team A.
choice_c: The penalty for the foul by B66 is subject to postscrimmage kick rules.
choice_d: The basic spot is the previous spot, the B-45.
explanation: B66's holding during the scrimmage kick that crossed the NZ is governed by post-scrimmage-kick enforcement, so it does not produce a safety even though B21 was downed in the end zone.

question_text: Because of a blinding snowstorm, the game is suspended with eight minutes remaining in the third quarter. The head coaches, game management and the Referee all agree that it will be impossible to resume the game. The two teams are members of different conferences. Which of the following is NOT possible by rule?
choice_a: The game may resume at a later date.
choice_b: The home team’s conference commissioner decides unilaterally that the game is to be terminated with the final score being the score at the time of the suspension.
choice_c: The game is declared a no-contest.
explanation: A suspended game that cannot resume is handled by conference/institutional agreement or declared no-contest; a single conference commissioner cannot unilaterally set the final score, so that option is NOT possible by rule.

tags: penalty on the try; neutral zone infraction; facemask; offsetting;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: On a try attempt at the B-3, Team B is flagged for lining up in the neutral zone at the snap. B80 blocks the kick, recovers the ball, and returns it until A18 tackles him at the A-45 by grasping and twisting his facemask.
choice_c: The penalty for A18’s foul is carried over to the kickoff.
explanation: On the try, B's neutral-zone foul and A18's facemask are fouls by both teams during the down - they offset and the try is repeated.

tags: defensive signaling; holding; pass interference; forward pass;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Second and 15 at the B-45. A12 throws a legal forward pass to the right side intended for eligible A88 at the B-38, but the pass is incomplete. During the pass B41 holds eligible A44 in the left flat at the B-40.
choice_a: Defensive holding. Ten-yard penalty at the previous spot. Second and five at the B-35. 23
choice_b: Defensive pass interference. The ball is placed at the spot of the foul, automatic first down.
choice_c: Defensive holding. Ten-yard penalty at the previous spot. First and 10 at the B-35.
explanation: Grabbing/holding a receiver away from the throw is defensive holding, not pass interference: 10 yards from the previous spot with an automatic first down - first-and-10 at the B-35.

question_text: Fourth and 10 at the A-20. A21 is in deep punt formation to receive a snap. At the A-6, A21 muffs the ball and, to prevent the defense from recovering, he bats the ball backward. It goes into the end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Foul by A21, illegally batting the ball.
choice_b: The result of the play is a safety.
choice_c: Team B’s ball, first and goal at the A-6.
explanation: A21 muffs the snap in his end zone and illegally bats the ball backward over the end line; Team A supplies the impetus, so the result is a safety (the illegal-bat foul is offset by accepting the safety).

question_text: Fourth and four at the B-10. B77 is in the neutral zone at the snap but does not make contact. The ball carrier is tackled inbounds at the B-7.
choice_a: First and goal at the B-5. Clock starts on the snap.
choice_b: First and goal at the B-5. Clock starts on the Referee’s signal.
explanation: B77's offside without contact is a 5-yard live-ball foul; first-and-goal at the B-5 with the clock starting on the referee's ready signal.

question_text: Fourth and 10 at the B-40. Receiver B30 muffs a punt at the B-15 and the ball rolls into the end zone. A25 and B41 simultaneously recover the ball in the end zone.
choice_b: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-20.
choice_c: Touchback. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-25.
choice_d: Safety. Two points for Team A.
explanation: B30 muffs the punt and the ball rolls into the end zone where A and B recover simultaneously; a simultaneous recovery of a kick in the receiving team's end zone is awarded to Team B - touchback, first-and-10 at the B-20.

tags: punt; illegal touching; holding; penalty on the try;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and 10 at the A-40. The punt crosses the neutral zone and A34 is the first to touch the ball at the B-15. B23 recovers the ball at the B-17 and runs to the B-21 where he fumbles. While the ball is loose from the fumble B92 holds A46 at the B-18. A37 recovers the ball at the B-20 and returns it across Team B’s goal line.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. The penalty is enforced on the try or the kickoff.
choice_b: Team A must decline the penalty in order for the touchdown to count.
choice_c: No score. Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-9.
explanation: A's first touching only entitles B to that spot; here A returns the loose ball for a touchdown, so the touchdown counts and B92's holding is enforced on the try or succeeding kickoff.

tags: out of bounds; illegal use of hands; sideline interference;2015-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Fourth and five at the B-15. On a sweep play to the right, cornerback B27 is legally blocked and is forced out of bounds at the B-11. While he is still out of bounds, B27 grabs and tries to tackle ball carrier A33 at the B-11, but A33 breaks loose and steps on the sideline at the B-5. 24
choice_a: First and goal for Team A at the B-5.
choice_b: Team B’s ball, first and 10 at the B-11.
explanation: B27 was legally forced out of bounds, so his attempt to tackle A33 from out of bounds is a foul (illegal participation from out of bounds); Team A gets the ball first-and-goal at the B-5 after the runner's progress.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. QB A12 throws a long pass down the sideline intended for A88 that is incomplete at the B-10. There was contact between B21 and A88 before the catchable pass arrived, but there was no call by the crew on the field. As the officials are coming back into their pre-snap positions, the Team A Offensive Coordinator shows the SJ the replay of the pass and player contact on the sideline utilizing the team's tablet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25, A-45, Snap. Using sideline video/tablet technology to show officials or players a replay during the game is an automatic dead-ball Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul. Team A is penalized 15 yards as a dead ball foul, and the Team A Offensive Coordinator receives a UNS counter.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 2nd quarter, QB A12 throws a desperation pass that is caught by A88 in the end zone for a Touchdown. The game clock expired during the play. The CJ has a flag down for 2 players on Team A with the unbranded green dot on the back middle line of the helmet indicating that the helmets were equipped with Coach to Player communications.
choice_a: Touchdown stands. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
choice_b: A, 2/15, B-35, No clock - Extend the 2nd quarter.
choice_d: A, 2/15, B-30, No clock - Extend the 2nd quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, No clock - Extend the 2nd quarter. If more than 1 green dot helmet is detected by the crew, the result is a live ball 5-yard equipment violation penalty. The 2nd quarter is extended for an untimed down since the penalty is accepted for a live ball foul on the last play of the 2nd quarter.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, ball carrier A44 runs off right tackle, reverses field and is tackled inbounds at the B-32. B55's helmet came off during the play and the clock is stopped at 1:58. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40 and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap. Play clock set to 40 seconds and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
choice_c: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap. Referee stops the game for the Two-Minute Timeout. Play clock set to 25 seconds and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-32, Ready. Play clock set to 25 seconds and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap. Referee stops the game for the Two-Minute Timeout. Play clock set to 25 seconds and B55 must leave the game for 1 play. There is no option for a 10-second runoff since the helmet came off before the clock got inside the Two-Minute Timeout mark. The play clock is always set to 25 seconds following the Two-Minute Timeout and unless Team B calls a team timeout, B55 must leave the game for 1 play.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Following the Two-Minute Timeout in the 2nd quarter, ball carrier A44 runs off right tackle, reverses field and is tackled inbounds at the B-32. B55's helmet came off during the play and the clock is stopped at 1:07. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap. Play clock set to 25 seconds and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 25 and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
choice_c: A, 3/12, B-32, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40 and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
