choice_d: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40 and B55 must leave the game for 1 play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40 and B55 must leave the game for 1 play. Since the helmet off is the only reason that the clock was stopped after the Two-Minute Timeout, a 10-Second Runoff applies. Team A may decline the runoff and have the clock start on the snap. Team B can call a team timeout to keep B55 in the game and avoid the runoff.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. QB A12 drops straight back to pass and cannot find a receiver open. A12 begins to scramble, never leaves the tackle box and B54 grabs the inside back collar of A12's jersey and abruptly pulls him down at the A-23.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. For player safety, horse-collar tackles that occur within the tackle box are fouls. The penalty is enforced 15 yards from the previous spot and carries an automatic 1st down.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. Late in the 2nd quarter and neither team has a timeout, A12 completes a pass to A88 at the B-20 where he is downed. With the clock running down, Team A rushes their field goal unit onto the field, and all Team A players are set in field goal formation and A90 kicks the ball for a successful field goal. Time expires during the kick. After clearing the play with the crew on the field, both teams leave the playing enclosure and replay gives the all clear as well. The Referee declares the half ended. During the halftime intermission, the instant replay official looks back and determines that Team A had 12 players on the field at the snap. Team A is scheduled to kickoff for the 2nd half.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. After the Referee has declared the first half ended, there can be no additional replay reviews from the previous play. The only exception is a halftime Targeting video review in games without Instant Replay.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-15. A89 enters the game and lines up at the left Tight End. QB A12 fakes a handoff, rolls out and spots A89 in the endzone and throws a pass that is complete in the endzone. The Team B coach complains that there is no number 89 on the game day roster and A89 competed in previous weeks as A79. A89 did not report to the Referee upon entering the game and Replay confirms over O2O that there is no 89 on the game day roster. The UNS is a counter for A89, and if he receives a second UNS he is ejected.
choice_a: Touchdown stands. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25, B-30, Snap. A player who enters the game after changing their number or with a different number than is on the game day roster and does not report to the Referee commits a foul for unsportsmanlike conduct. The live ball foul is penalized 15 yards from the previous spot, nullifying the touchdown. The clock starts on the snap since the result of the play was a touchdown.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. Team A snaps from the left hash, opposite their team area. A81 is flanked out to the left side of the formation and goes in motion back toward the ball. After the snap, A21 takes the handoff and runs for 3-yards behind the left tackle. A81 never breaks stride and is inside the hash when the ball is declared dead and continues toward the Team A team area simulating leaving the field. A81 sets near the sideline for a "hide-out" pass. Team A does not substitute and just after the next snap, A12 throws a TD pass to A81 who is running just inside the boundary.
choice_a: Touchdown stands. A, Try, A-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/22, A-28, Snap. Team A commits a foul at the snap for unsportsmanlike conduct: using the substitution process to confuse opponents, with or without an actual substitution. This live-ball Team UNS foul is penalized 15 yards from the previous spot, nullifying the touchdown. The clock starts on the snap since the result of the play was a touchdown.

question_text: 3/10 @ 50. With 2:02 remaining in the 2nd quarter, receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline and runs to the B-17 where he is tackled in bounds (1:50). The officials spot the ball there. Replays show after making the catch A80 stepped out of bounds at the B-30 (1:55).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-30, 1:50, Snap. Whether A80 stepped out of bounds is reviewable and the ball should be spotted at the B-30 after review. There is no adjustment to the game clock since the play began before the Two-Minute Timeout. Clock starts on the snap following the Two-Minute Timeout.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35. With 1:35 remaining in the 4th quarter the score is tied at 24 and both teams have two timeouts remaining. QB A1 scrambles out of the pocket to the A-28 and as he is being tackled by B56 throws a pass out of bounds at the A-40 that is ruled incomplete (1:26). Replays show A1's left knee down before he released the ball. There was 1:31 on the game clock when the knee was down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/17, A-28, 10-Second Runoff. Replay determines A1 was down by contact before the pass, so it reverts to a run ending at the A-28. Reset clock to 1:31 and run 10 seconds unless either team takes a timeout to avoid the runoff. If no timeout is taken, the clock goes to 1:21 and starts on Ready; if a timeout is taken, the clock stays at 1:31 and starts on Snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. Late in the 4th quarter with 2:03 remaining at the snap, A12 drops back to pass, scrambles and runs to the B-18 where he is tackled inbounds with 1:55 remaining on the clock. During the play, B55's helmet came completely off (not as a direct result of a foul). Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/3, B-18, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40 seconds and B55 must leave the game for one play.
choice_b: A, 3/3, B-18, Two-Minute Timeout. Play clock set to 40 Seconds and B55 must leave the game for one play.
choice_c: A, 3/3, B-18, Two-Minute Timeout. Play clock set to 25 Seconds and B55 must leave the game for one play.
choice_d: A, 3/13, B-18, Two-Minute Timeout. Play clock set to 25 Seconds and B55 must leave the game for one play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/3, B-18, Two-Minute Timeout. Play clock set to 25 Seconds and B55 must leave the game for one play. The ball is live when the clock gets to and runs inside of 2 minutes, so the Referee or covering official stops the clock immediately following the down for the Two-Minute Timeout. B55 must leave the game for one play unless the defense takes a team timeout. The play clock is set to 25 seconds after the Two-Minute Timeout.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B21 gives the "T signal" as the kick is in flight. B21 catches the kick right at the goal line and returns the kick back to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. The "T signal", if executed properly, is not considered a valid or invalid fair catch signal. It does not cause the ball to become dead and does not deprive the receiving team the opportunity to advance the ball.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Early in the 4th quarter, back A21 takes a handoff and runs for a 15-yard gain and is down inbounds. During the play, A88 is guilty of holding at the A-30, and B54's helmet comes completely off (not as a direct result of a foul). Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_b: A, 1/15, A-20, Snap. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_c: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 25 seconds.
choice_d: A, 1/10, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Play clock set to 40 seconds. The holding foul by A88 is penalized 10 yards from the A-30, the spot of the foul. The play clock is set to 40 because of the helmet off by B54, and both the play clock and game clock start on the Referee's signal.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 3/2 @ B-15. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, A45 fumbles after gaining three yards. The officials cannot determine who has recovered the fumble, so the Line Judge signals the clock to stop while the ball is being located. A45 is found to be in possession of the ball at the B-14 and has not made the line to gain. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 4/1, B-14, Ready. Play clock reset to 40 seconds regardless.
choice_b: A, 4/1, B-14, Running. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-14, Running. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
choice_d: A, 4/1, B-15, Running. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-14, Running. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready. A45's recovery is short of the line to gain. The 40-second clock starts when the ball is declared dead, and the Referee immediately signals the game clock to start when the recovery is determined. If the play clock is less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready, the Referee resets it to 25 seconds.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 3/2 @ B-15. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, A45 fumbles after gaining three yards. The officials cannot determine who has recovered the fumble, so the Line Judge signals the clock to stop while the ball is being located. A45 is found to be in possession of the ball at the B-12 and has made the line to gain. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-12, Running. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
choice_b: A, 4/1, B-12, Ready. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-12, Ready. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-14, Ready. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-12, Ready. Play clock reset only if less than 25 seconds when the ball is ready. The 40-second clock starts when the ball is declared dead. The game clock starts on the Referee's signal when the ball is ready for play, since the line to gain was made. If the play clock is less than 25 seconds when the Referee is ready to signal, it is reset to 25 seconds.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, Team A has all 3 timeouts remaining. A12 throws to A88 but the pass is ruled incomplete. The Team A head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to a catch at the B-31. After the ball is ready at the B-31, Team A breaks the huddle late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach attempts to call a timeout just before the play clock displays zero.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Dead-ball foul, delay of game by Team A. Team A had 4/1 @ B-31 after the overturn, then 4/6 following the 5-yard delay of game penalty. The officials will not honor the request for a second timeout. Team A retains their challenge (usable once more) and retains their timeout, keeping 3 timeouts remaining in the 1st half.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the fourth quarter and the clock running with 1:25 left in the game, Team B is behind by 3 points and has no timeouts remaining. To conserve time, B77 crosses the neutral zone and touches a Team A player.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/5, A-30, 10-Second Runoff. B77 commits a dead-ball foul for offside, a five-yard penalty from the succeeding spot. Because this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, this foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule and the clock starts on the ready for play.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter and the clock running, Team A has no timeouts remaining. Ball carrier A21 sweeps around right end and throws a backward pass out of bounds to conserve time from the B-25 and the ball goes out of bounds at the B-27. The clock reads 0:15 when the ball lands out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, B-30, 10-Second Runoff. A21 commits a foul for an illegal pass; the penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul and loss of down. Because this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, this foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule and the game clock is reset to 0:05, starting on the Referee's signal.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-25. Returner B3 signals for a fair catch (valid) but muffs A1's punt at the B-30 and the ball is immediately recovered by A5 on the ground at the B-27. The Back Judge ruled that B3 caught the ball and killed the play blowing the whistle. The Side Judge had a flag down for holding by B14 during the kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-27, Snap. Whether the ball was possessed or muffed is reviewable, and since there is a clear recovery in the continuing action, replay gives Team A possession at that spot. Team A will decline the holding penalty. If there was no clear recovery, the ruling on the field would stand and the penalty would use postscrimmage kick enforcement.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. A1's pass is caught at the B-41 by A3 who runs to the B-5 where he is tackled in bounds. The Side Judge has a flag down and reports to the Referee offensive pass interference by A14 for blocking downfield before the ball was caught.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-5, Ready. Where the ball was touched in relation to the line of scrimmage is reviewable. Replay informs the Referee via O2O that the ball was first touched behind the line of scrimmage, so there is no foul for offensive pass interference.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Late in the 1st quarter, ball carrier A21 circles right end toward his team area and is hit by B54 at the A-30. A21 continues to drive forward and lands with his body down at the A-34 out of bounds in the white. There are no substitutions for Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/1, A-34, Ready. The game clock is stopped and restarts on the Referee's signal. The play clock is set to 40. When Team A players go out of bounds after the play between the twenty-yard lines on their team area's side of the field, the Referee will initiate the "T" and work through substitution mechanics.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-30. Following the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, ball carrier A22 runs and goes out of bounds at the B-18. B88 commits a dead ball personal foul immediately after the ball is out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-9, Snap. The penalty for any dead ball foul that occurs after a series ends and before the ball is ready for play shall be completed before the line to gain is established. When the ball carrier is out of bounds, the clock starts on the snap any time after the Two-Minute Timeout in either half.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the game with the score tied, Team A lines up to free kick and Kicker A10 places the ball down at the right hash for an apparent onside kick. After the Referee's ready-for-play signal, A10 approaches the ball and then picks up the ball, runs to the left hash at the A-35, places the ball down, and quickly kicks the ball.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, Free Kick Rules.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-30, Free Kick Rules.
choice_d: B, Free Kick, B-30, Free Kick Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, Free Kick Rules. By interpretation, once the ball position between the hash marks has been selected by Team A and after the ready-for-play, the ball may be relocated only after a charged team timeout or before any re-kick. This action results in a dead-ball foul, a 5-yard penalty from the succeeding spot.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. With the game clock running, defender B70 is lined up with his right hand and helmet in the neutral zone. As the play clock winds down, restricted lineman A77 reaches out and touches B70.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15, A-20, Ready. Dead ball foul, false start by A77. With the defense lined up in the neutral zone and no immediate reaction by the offense, the offense subsequently moving to touch or contact the defender is a false start on the offense.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, the ball is snapped over the head of quarterback A12, who is in the shotgun formation. A12 recovers the ball and immediately throws it forward into the ground to conserve time at the B-40. The clock reads 1:01 after the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/20, B-40, 10-Second Subtraction. This is an illegal forward pass for intentional grounding; not a valid attempt to conserve time. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the foul. Because this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, this foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule. Reset the clock to 0:51 and start on the Referee's signal.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. Defender B21 is airborne near the sideline when he gains firm control of a legal forward pass and lands with both feet inbounds at the A-45 and is down. When B21 jumped from the sideline area, his left heel was out of bounds, and he jumped with a single process with the heel coming off the ground just before the toes.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-30, Snap. If the toe or heel is out of bounds when a player starts a continuous heel-toe or toe-heel process of jumping, the player is out of bounds and has not re-established inbounds. Airborne B21 has not re-established inbounds and remains an out of bounds player, so the pass is incomplete.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, quarterback A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the B-35 that falls incomplete after ineligible A58 touches the ball in an attempt to catch it at the line of scrimmage. There were no eligible receivers in the area. The clock reads 0:25 after the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. A10 commits a foul for intentional grounding. There is no foul for illegal touching since the pass is illegal. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass. Because this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, this foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule; the clock is reset to 0:15 and starts on the Referee's signal.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, quarterback A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the B-35 that falls incomplete after ineligible A58 touches the ball in an attempt to catch it at the line of scrimmage. There were no eligible receivers in the area. The clock reads 0:00 after the play.
choice_c: A, 3/15, B-35, No Clock - Extend the 2nd quarter.
choice_d: Half Over, at the option of Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Half Over, at the option of Team B. A10 commits a foul for intentional grounding. There is no foul for illegal touching since the pass is illegal. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass. Because the statement of the penalty includes loss of down, the period is not extended at the option of the offended team, Team B.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. QB A1 scrambles toward the sideline and attempts to throw the ball away out of bounds. B3 who is standing near the sideline at the B-25 jumps and controls the pass while airborne then comes down with his right foot in bounds before stepping out of bounds with his left foot while maintaining control of the ball. The Side Judge rules the pass incomplete. Replays show B3 with his right foot in bounds and his left heel touching the sideline before he steps back into the field of play with his left foot then leaves both feet to attempt to catch the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Whether B3 was in or out of bounds is reviewable. B3 is out of bounds when his left foot is touching the sideline, but re-establishes inbounds when he brings that foot off the sideline and back down in the field of play. He completes the catch, so the ruling is overturned to an interception.

question_text: 3/10 @ 50. Near the end of the first half Team A is out of timeouts. Receiver A80 catches a pass at the B-38 and runs to the B-30 and time expires. Replays show A80's right knee was down at the B-30 with 0:02 remaining on the game clock.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-30, No Clock - Extend the half.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Half Over. Whether there was time remaining on the game clock when the down ended is reviewable, but since there is less than three seconds on the clock and Team A is out of timeouts by rule, the half is over. If three or more seconds remained, replay would adjust the clock and it would start on the ready for play; if Team A had a timeout, replay would adjust the clock and charge the timeout, clock on snap.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. Punter A98 is 15 yards behind the neutral zone when he catches the long snap, sprints to his right and runs outside the tackle box. He then stops and punts the ball and is immediately hit by a diving B89 at the A-30. The punt rolls out of bounds at the B-25.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-30, Snap. Roughing the kicker on B89.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Legal play, no foul by B89. A98 loses his roughing or running-into protection by carrying the ball outside the tackle box.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-40. Punter A98 is 15 yards behind the neutral zone when he catches the long snap, sprints to his right and runs outside the tackle box. He then stops and punts the ball and is immediately hit by a diving B89 at the A-30 who makes forcible contact to A98 with the crown of the helmet. The punt rolls out of bounds at the B-25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-45, Snap. Disqualify B89. A98 loses his roughing or running-into protection by carrying the ball outside the tackle box. However, even though the kicker is outside the tackle box, targeting with the crown of the helmet is a personal foul against the kicker and the penalty is enforced at the previous spot. B89 is disqualified.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-30. Team A's punt is blocked behind the neutral zone and while the ball is loose, B54 holds A88 at the A-32. The ball never crosses the neutral zone during the play and B75 recovers the ball at the A-25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Snap. Postscrimmage Kick Enforcement applies only when a scrimmage kick crosses the neutral zone. B54's holding foul is enforced from the previous spot since it occurred during the kick. Team A retains possession after the penalty is enforced at the previous spot, and the penalty statement includes an automatic first down.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-30. A98's punt crosses the neutral zone and B44 catches the kick at the B-25 and returns to the B-40 where he is tackled. During the kick, B79 holds A55 one yard beyond the neutral zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Team B keeps possession of the ball. B79's holding foul is governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement; the 10-yard penalty is enforced at the end of the kick, which is the B-25.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-30. A98's punt crosses the neutral zone and B44 catches the kick at the B-25 and returns to the B-40 where he fumbles and A88 recovers. During the kick, B79 holds A55 one yard beyond the neutral zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Snap. B79's holding foul is not governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement since A88 recovered the fumble and Team B will not next put the ball in play. Team A will decline B79's holding penalty and take the result of the play, giving them 1st & 10 at the B-40.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. B44 intercepts the pass of A12 at the B-20 and turns back up-field on the return. During the return, B21 approaches A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocks A88 with extended hands. B44 returns the pass to the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-20, Snap. B21 does not commit a foul for an illegal blind side block because he executes the blind side block with extended hands.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. B44 intercepts the pass of A12 at the B-20 and turns back up-field on the return. During the return, B21 approaches A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocks A88 by attacking with forcible contact with his shoulder into the chest of A88. B44 returns the pass to the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. B21 is guilty of an illegal blind side block, approaching A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocking with forcible contact. The foul occurred after the change of possession, so Team B keeps the ball and the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, midfield.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. B44 intercepts the pass of A12 at the B-20 and turns back up-field on the return. During the return, B21 approaches A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocks A88 by attacking with forcible contact with the shoulder into the head of A88. B44 returns the pass to the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. Disqualify B21. B21 is guilty of an illegal blind side block with Targeting, attacking A88's head and neck area from the blind side. A88 is a defenseless player because he receives a blind side block. The foul occurred after the change of possession, so Team B keeps the ball and the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, midfield. B21 is disqualified.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. Late in the 2nd quarter, receiver A80 controls A1's deep pass at the B-2 and goes to the ground in the end zone. The Side Judge rules TD (0:23). Replays show after gaining control A80's left knee hit the ground and the ball was at the B-1 (0:25) before he completed the catch in the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown stands. A, Try, B-1, No Clock.
choice_c: A, 1/Goal, B-1, reset clock to 0:25, Ready.
choice_d: A, 1/Goal, B-2, reset clock to 0:25, Ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-1, reset clock to 0:25, Ready. Whether A80 caught the pass and the ball in relation to the goal line are reviewable aspects of the play. A80 completes the process of the catch but is down by rule when his knee touches the ground after control is established. The clock is reset since it is after the two-minute timeout and the line to gain was made; there is no runoff since the clock stops when the line to gain is reached.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-35. On the first play after the two-minute timeout, QB A1 is tackled at the B-40 and the ball comes loose (1:55). B56 recovers and runs to the A-40 where he is tackled in bounds (1:48). The officials rule fumble. Replays show A1's right knee down before the ball came loose.
choice_a: B, 1/10, A-40, reset clock to 1:48, Snap.
choice_b: A, 3/10, B-40, reset clock to 1:55, Ready.
choice_c: A, 2/10, B-40, reset clock to 1:55 and 10-second runoff rule applies.
choice_d: A, 3/10, B-40, reset clock to 1:55 and 10-second runoff rule applies.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-40, reset clock to 1:55 and 10-second runoff rule applies. Whether A1 was down or not is reviewable. Since this play takes place after the two-minute timeout and A1 was down short of the line to gain, the clock would have continued to run, so the runoff rule applies. Either team can take a remaining timeout to avoid the runoff; if the runoff applies, the clock resets to 1:45 and starts on ready, and if a timeout is taken, the clock stays at 1:55 and starts on snap.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98's free kick is caught at the goal line and B44 returns the kick to the B-25. Team A had 3 green dot helmets on the kickoff indicating that 3 Team A players on the field had receiving capability for coach-to-player communications.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. 5-yard equipment penalty on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. On free kick plays, the coach-to-player communications limit is not in effect. There is no limitation to the number of green dot helmets for either team during free kick plays.

question_text: 4/2 @ B-40. Team A QB A12 is in shotgun formation and the backup QB A10 is positioned as a wideout. A12 hands the ball to A44 and he gains 4 yards on the play. Both A12 and A10 have green dot helmets indicating that they have receiving capability for coach-to-player communications.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/7, B-45, Ready. If more than one green dot helmet per team is detected on the field, the result is a live ball 5-yard equipment violation penalty, enforced from the previous spot. This penalty also initiates a conference review.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. A12 throws a legal forward pass, and receiver A88 first touches the pass when the ball is 1 1/2 yards beyond the neutral zone. The receiver's back foot is not beyond the neutral zone. A88 runs to the A-35 after the catch, and A77 was at the A-29 when A88 first touched the pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15, A-20, Ready. The guiding principle on a pass crossing the neutral zone is the location of the ball when it is first touched, not the player's position. Since the pass crossed the neutral zone before A88 touched it, A77 has committed a foul for ineligible receiver downfield.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-5. A55's legal snap is muffed by A12 and A21 recovers at the B-7 and advances the ball into the end zone.
choice_a: Illegal advance - no score. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, Running Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown. A, Try, B-5, No Clock.
choice_d: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock. The snap is a backward pass and not a fumble. There is no restriction on a Team A player recovering and advancing a backward pass on 4th down. The recovery and advance by A21 is legal.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. A81 catches A12's forward pass at the B-35 and fumbles at the B-31. The fumble is recovered by grounded A84 five yards in advance of the fumble. Just after releasing the pass, A12 was roughed by B54.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-13, Ready. The penalty for B54's roughing the passer foul is enforced from the B-26, the spot of recovery by A84 and the end of last run for Team A. The penalty is half the distance to the goal line and includes an automatic 1st down.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 3/Goal @ B-6. During the second possession series of the first extra period with the score tied, Team A decides to attempt a field goal on 3rd down. The field goal attempt is blocked, and the ball does not cross the neutral zone. A23 recovers the ball at the B-10, runs and is tackled at the B-3.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-3, No clock. Each team retains the ball during a possession series in extra periods until it scores or fails to make a first down. Because the kick never crossed the neutral zone, A23's recovery and advance is legal. Since it was 3rd down and Team A advanced but did not make a first down, it is Team A's ball on 4th down.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-15. Time expires in the first half on a play in which A12 is completely beyond the neutral zone at the B-13 when he throws and completes a pass to A88 in Team B's end zone.
choice_b: No score. A, 3/15, B-15, Untimed down.
choice_d: No score, penalty declined by Team B. Half over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No score. Half over. Team B accepts the penalty to nullify the score. At the option of Team B, the second quarter is not extended because the statement of the penalty for illegal forward pass includes loss of down. The first half ends.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. Late in the fourth quarter, with Team A trailing 20-17, Quarterback A10 rolls out and is at the B-12 when he throws a forward pass that is incomplete. When the ball is dead the game clock shows 0:13.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/7, B-17, 10-Second Runoff. Team B accepts the penalty for the illegal forward pass; the 5-yard penalty is enforced from the B-12, the spot of the foul. Team B has the option for a 10-second runoff since the foul occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout. If accepted, the game clock is set at 0:03 and starts on the Referee's signal.

question_text: 3/3 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter Team A leads 38-10 and both teams have one timeout remaining. Back A2 runs up the middle and gets tackled near the line to gain (1:24). The ball is spotted at the B-37 for a 1st down and the game clock is wound on the ready for play. The Team B coach then challenges that A2 was down with the ball short of the line to gain. Replays show A2's right knee down with the ball at the B-38.
choice_a: A, 4/1, B-38, reset clock to 1:24, 10-Second Runoff.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-37, reset clock to 1:24, Ready.
choice_c: A, 4/1, B-38, reset clock to 1:24, Ready.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-37, reset clock to 1:24, 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-38, reset clock to 1:24, 10-Second Runoff. Team B wins the challenge and the ball is short of the line to gain. Team B keeps their timeout, which they can use to avoid the runoff — an exception to the consecutive timeout rule. If the score were close, the Referee should stop the game rather than wait for the coach to challenge.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the second quarter the score is tied, and Team A has no timeouts remaining. Receiver A80 catches a pass and runs to the B-20 where he is tackled in bounds and time expires. The Referee gets on the mic immediately and instructs the clock operator to put one second back on the game clock. The line feed shows when A80 was down there were three seconds on the clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, 0:03, Ready. Replay should use O2O in this situation to inform the Referee of the correct time remaining. This should not go to a full review. There would have to be three or more seconds remaining for this to have a significant competitive effect.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score late in the 4th quarter. When ball carrier A22 is tackled in the field of play at the A-35, the game clock reads 1:47. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-26.
choice_a: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap (at the option of Team B).
choice_b: A, 2/16, A-35, Snap (at the option of Team B).
choice_d: A, 2/26, A-26, Snap (at the option of Team B).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap (at the option of Team B). A55's holding foul is enforced 10 yards from the spot of the foul, the A-26. After enforcement, the game clock starts on the snap or on the referee's signal, at the option of Team B.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. The game clock is running late in the 2nd half. Team A trails by two points and is out of timeouts. After the ball is ready for play lineman A66 commits a false start, and when the officials stop the game clock it reads 8 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. Team B accepts the yardage penalty and the clock runoff. Since there are only 8 seconds remaining, after the clock runoff the game is over. Team B wins.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. The game clock is running late in the 2nd half. Team A trails by two points and is out of timeouts. At the snap Team A has five players in the backfield. A22 carries for a ten-yard gain to the B-20. When the ball is declared dead the game clock reads 8 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, Ready. Illegal formation against Team A results in a 5-yard penalty from the previous spot. Because illegal formation is not a foul that causes the clock to stop immediately, the 10-second runoff does not apply. The game clock starts on the referee's signal at the option of Team B.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. Late in the 4th quarter with the game clock running, Tackle B77 is in the neutral zone at the snap but does not make contact. QB A12 rolls out to pass, runs to the B-17 and throws a forward pass to conserve time, which falls incomplete. The game clock reads 0:15.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-20, Ready. B77 is offside and A12's pass is an illegal forward pass to conserve time. These are offsetting fouls, so there is no 10-second runoff. The game clock remains at 15 seconds and starts on the referee's signal.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QB A12 takes the snap and rolls out to the right. Finding no receiver open, A12 turns up field and runs. After making the line to gain, A12 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet first at the B-27, stays upright and runs for a touchdown.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-27, Running. The live ball becomes dead at the point where A12 simulates or fakes as if he will begin a feet-first slide. An official shall sound their whistle and declare the ball dead. Team A will have 1st & 10 at the B-27. A fake slide is not reviewable.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. In the 3rd quarter, A88 is airborne and secures firm control of A12's forward pass. A88's right foot lands inbounds, and he maintains firm control of the ball as he goes to the ground inbounds at the A-45. Player B21, who was standing out of bounds, had his hand touching the ball while A88 was airborne and in firm control of the ball at the A-44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Running. A88 has completed all the elements of a catch. Since the ball is in player control by A88, the ball is not considered out of bounds when it is touched by B21, who was standing out of bounds.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-1. As the snapper A55 takes his position with the clock running early in the 4th quarter, he moves the ball forward and the nose of the football breaks the plane of the goal line. After the snap, QB A12 hands the ball to A44 who dives into the end zone.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-1, No Clock.
choice_b: A, 4/Goal, B-1, Snap. A55 and the head coach are issued a warning.
choice_c: A, 4/Goal, B-1, Ready. A55 and the head coach are issued a warning.
choice_d: A, 4/Goal+5, Ready. False Start on A55.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-1, Ready. A55 and the head coach are issued a warning. The snapper shall not be permitted to move the ball more than 1/2 yard forward when taking his stance, nor allow the ball to be moved over the line to gain or goal line. The play shall be shut down, the ball reset, and the player and his coach warned. A foul for a False Start is considered only if A55 continues to slide the ball forward after the warning.

question_text: 4/5 @ 50. A98's punt is untouched beyond the neutral zone when A88 reaches across Team B's goal line and bats the ball back into the field of play. B21 recovers at the B-2 and advances to the B-12 where A66 tackles him by pulling his facemask.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-27, Snap. A88's batting of the ball in the end zone is illegal touching, with no foul for batting. Team B will accept the penalty for the facemask foul, which cancels the illegal touching privilege, and the 15-yard penalty enforced from the end of B21's run gives a better starting spot than the illegal touching spot at Team B's 20.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-15. Late in the 2nd quarter, Back A2 runs near the sideline to the B-12 where he is hit, and the ball comes loose (0:43). B56 jumps on the loose ball at the sideline at the B-9 (0:40). The officials rule fumble and B56 recovered the ball inbounds awarding possession to Team B. Replays show A2 did fumble and that B56's left foot was on the sideline when he first touched the loose ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/7, B-12, 0:40, Ready. Whether A2 fumbled and the recovery at the sideline are reviewable aspects of the play. Since B56 was out of bounds when he touched the loose ball, the ball is out of bounds at that spot and goes back to Team A, and since that spot is in advance of the spot of the fumble, the ball goes back to the spot of the fumble. There is no runoff since the review results in a stopped clock.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. A1's punt is caught by B3 at the B-10 who returns it to the B-40 where he is tackled inbounds. There is a flag on the field and the Referee announces that during the kick there was a blindside block with targeting by B14. Replays show that B14 led with his shoulder and made forcible contact to the shoulder of the kicking team player at the B-30. The contact occurred clearly after B3 caught the ball and started running with it.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-15, no DQ, Snap. The blindside block is reviewable for the targeting aspect only, and a penalty is enforced against Team B regardless of the outcome of the review. Since there was no contact with the crown or to the head/neck area, there is no targeting and B14 remains in the game. Replay informs the Referee the foul occurred during the return, and the crew enforces the penalty accordingly.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the game, A22 runs for 8 yards and is tackled short of the line to gain. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54's helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff (Play clock set to 40).
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap (Play clock set to 40).
choice_c: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap (Play clock set to 25).
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-32, Ready (Play clock set to 25).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap (Play clock set to 25). The game clock is stopped at 1:58 for the Two-Minute Timeout and for B54's helmet being off. Even with a Team B helmet off, the play clock will be set to 25 seconds following the Two-Minute Timeout, and the game clock will start on the snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, A22 runs for 8 yards and is tackled short of the line to gain, and the Team A head coach calls timeout at the end of the play. B54's helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap (Play clock set to 40).
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff (Play clock set to 25).
choice_c: A, 3/2, B-32, Ready (Play clock set to 25).
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap (Play clock set to 25).
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-32, Snap (Play clock set to 25). The game clock is stopped for B54's helmet being off and the timeout for Team A. Even with a Team B helmet off, the play clock will be set to 25 seconds following a called team timeout, and the game clock will start on the snap.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 4/5 @ B-10. During the first possession series of the 1st extra period, Team A's field goal attempt is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. A80 recovers the ball at the B-15 and runs to the B-4 where he is downed.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-4, No Clock. Team A retains the ball and continues its possession series since A80 made the line to gain.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 4/5 @ B-10. During the first possession series of the 1st extra period, Team A's field goal attempt is blocked and does not cross the neutral zone. B55 recovers the ball at the B-15 and runs to the B-40 where he fumbles. A80 recovers the ball at the B-40 and runs to the B-4 where he is downed.
choice_b: Possession Series Ended. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_d: Possession Series Ended. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, Ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Possession Series Ended. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. Team A may not have a first down if it regains possession after a change of team possession. Team B will begin their possession series at the 25-yard line.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the 1st extra period, B21 intercepts A12's pass at the B-4, reverses field on the return and is downed two yards deep in his own end zone (no momentum).
choice_b: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, 1/10, 20-yard line, No Clock.
choice_c: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_d: Safety, 2 points for Team B. A, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock. B21 carries the ball into his own end zone, is responsible for the ball being there, and is downed there, resulting in a safety. Team A's possession series is over and Team B puts the ball in play, first and 10 on the 25-yard line, trailing by 2 points.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 4th quarter, B33 intercepts A12's pass at the B-10 and returns for a touchdown. As the Line Judge is coming down the sideline covering the play, they drop the flag during the return after running into the Team B head coach. The head coach was out on the field of play at the B-40.
choice_a: No Touchdown - Team UNS declined the score. B, 1/10, B-40, Snap.
choice_b: Touchdown for B. Try @ 3-yard line & Free Kick @ 20 (no choice for Team A).
choice_c: Touchdown for B. Try @ 18-yard line & Free Kick @ 20.
choice_d: Touchdown for B. Try @ 3-yard line & Free Kick @ 20 -- or -- Try @ 18-yard line & Free Kick @ 35 (Team A's choice).
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for B. Try @ 3-yard line & Free Kick @ 20 -- or -- Try @ 18-yard line & Free Kick @ 35 (Team A's choice). Although this foul takes place while the ball is alive, it is treated as a dead-ball foul. The touchdown counts, and the 15-yard penalty is enforced on the try or the succeeding kickoff, at the option of Team A. This is a team Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul.

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. Team A lines up in scrimmage kick formation and intends to punt. Immediately after the snap, B55 grabs RT A77 and pulls him to the side and B21 shoots the gap in an attempt to block the punt. The punter gets the punt away and the ball is downed at the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Snap. B55's action is holding. This foul occurred before the kick, so the penalty is not governed by Postscrimmage Kick Enforcement. The 10-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot and includes an automatic first down.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98's kick is muffed in flight by B21 in his end zone. While the ball is loose in the end zone, B21 bats the ball out of the back of the end zone.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap -- or -- A, Re-kick, A-45, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap -- or -- A, Re-kick, A-45, FK Rules. The result of the play is a touchback. The foul for illegally batting a ball in the end zone by B21 occurs during the kick, and the penalty enforcement would be 10 yards from the previous spot, the A-35.

question_text: 3/10 @ 50. QB A1 rolls out near the sideline and throws the ball toward receiver A80 at the B-38. The pass falls incomplete, but the SJ throws a flag for DPI on B23 who was covering A80. The Referee ruled A1 out of bounds at the A-47 before the pass was released and after a discussion the flag for DPI is picked up. Replays show A1 was not out of bounds before throwing the pass. The Referee announces there is no foul for pass interference as the ruling on the field is the QB was out of bounds before throwing the pass, and the previous play is under review.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-38, Snap. Whether A1 was out of bounds prior to the pass is reviewable, and replay can make this an incomplete pass. If the call is overturned, the flag for DPI goes back down and Team A has the option to accept the penalty, which they will do.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Receiver A80 catches a pass near the sideline at the A-40 and is downed there. The Field Judge has a hat off and throws the flag for illegal touching, ruling A80 went out of bounds on his own and re-established back in bounds before catching the pass. Replays show A80 had his left foot out of bounds and right foot in bounds initially, then got his left foot back down in bounds before touching the pass. There is also a replay showing B13 touching the pass in flight at the A-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. Whether A80 was out of bounds, re-established, and caught the pass is reviewable, as well as whether B13 touched the pass first. Since Team B touched it first, that makes A80 eligible, and he did re-establish when he got his left foot down in bounds.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A and Team B line up in position and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has two green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. A98's kick is fair caught by B21 at the B-5.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. 5-yard equipment penalty on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. There is no foul on the play. On free kick plays, the coach-to-player communications rule is not in effect. There is no limitation to the number of green dot helmets for either team during free kick plays.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-30. Ineligible lineman A70 runs five yards beyond the neutral zone and does not make contact with an opponent. A70 circles toward the flank and returns back across the neutral zone before A12 throws a legal forward pass caught by A88 at the 50. A88 runs to the B-40 where he is downed.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15, A-25, Ready. No originally ineligible receiver shall be or have been more than three yards beyond the neutral zone until a passer throws a legal forward pass that crosses the neutral zone. Since A70 went beyond three yards, even though he circled back, he has committed a foul for an ineligible receiver downfield. The penalty is 5 yards from the previous spot and does not include loss of down.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-10. A98's field goal attempt is partially blocked behind the neutral zone by B54, but continues in flight and hits the ground at the B-5. It then rebounds to the B-13 where B44 muffs it. A21 recovers while grounded at the B-11.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-11, Snap. Team B has touched a scrimmage kick that has crossed the neutral zone, so all players become eligible to legally recover the ball.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-45. QB A12 drops back and looks downfield for A88 who is the primary receiver and defender B21 is holding A88 at the B-25. A12 throws a forward pass to his checkdown receiver who makes the catch at the B-40 and is downed.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. The penalty for B21's holding foul is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot and includes an automatic 1st down.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-10. A98's untouched punt from his end zone goes out of bounds at Team A's 30-yard line. A21, in his end zone, grabs B55's face mask before the ball is kicked.
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team B and Team A, Free Kick @ A-20 -- or -- B, 1/10, A-15, Snap.
choice_c: Safety, 2 points for Team B and Team A, Free Kick @ A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 points for Team B and Team A, Free Kick @ A-20 -- or -- B, 1/10, A-15, Snap. Team B may accept the penalty for A21's foul, which occurred in the end zone and results in a safety and 2 points for Team B. Team B's other option is to snap the ball at Team A's 15-yard line after enforcement of the 15-yard penalty from the spot where the ball belongs to Team B, the A-30.

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A98 punts and the kick is untouched beyond the neutral zone when A88 reaches across Team B's goal line before the ball touches the ground in the end zone and bats it back into the field of play. B22 muffs the ball at the B-2 and A43 recovers it at the B-6. While the ball is loose B77 holds A21 at the B-10.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Snap. Postscrimmage kick rules do not apply to B77's foul since Team B will not next put the ball in play. Team A may cancel the illegal touching privilege by accepting B77's holding penalty, which is enforced at the previous spot with an automatic first down.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, team A has all 3 timeouts remaining. A12 throws to A88 but the pass is ruled incomplete. The Team A head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to a catch at the B-31. After the ball is ready at the B-31, Team A breaks the huddle late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach attempts to call a timeout just before the play clock displays zero.
choice_d: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Team A charged a 2nd timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Consecutive charged team timeouts are not allowed by the same team in any individual dead ball period, so the officials will not honor the request by the Team A coach. Team A had 4/1 @ B-31 after the overturn but incurs a 5-yard delay of game penalty. Team A retains their challenge and their timeout, keeping 3 timeouts remaining in the 1st half.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter following the Two-Minute Timeout, Team A is trailing by five points and has all 3 timeouts remaining. A12 throws to a diving A88 but the pass is ruled incomplete, and the game clock is stopped at 0:15. The Team A head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to a catch at the B-31.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1, B-31, 10-Second Runoff. Since the overturn was after the Two-Minute Timeout and the review resulted in the on-field ruling being overturned to a result that would not have stopped the game clock, the play is subject to a 10-second runoff. After a successful coach's challenge, either team may use a timeout to avoid the runoff.

question_text: 2/20 @ A-30. With 0:30 remaining in the 4th quarter, Team A trails 35-31 and has one timeout remaining. QB A1 scrambles to the A-32 where he throws a forward pass that is caught by A80 at the A-45 where he is tackled in bounds immediately. There are no flags on the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/23, A-27, Ready. Whether A1 was beyond the neutral zone when the pass was thrown is reviewable, and since his entire body and the ball were beyond it, replay creates a foul for an illegal forward pass. There is no runoff since the pass was completed, but the clock starts on the ready for play and Team A can use their remaining timeout to go on the snap.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-10. Receiver A80 controls a forward pass at the B-2, gets his right foot down then dives for the goal line. The ball comes loose when he hits the ground, and the Back Judge rules the pass incomplete. Replays are not clear whether the ball broke the plane of the goal line before A80 was down.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-1/2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-1/2, Ready. Whether the pass was complete or incomplete and the ball's relation to the goal line are both reviewable. A80 completes the catch by gaining control, getting a body part down, then performing an act common to the game. Since it is not clear whether the ball crossed the goal line, replay cannot make it a score, and the ball is placed at the appropriate spot short of the goal line.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. A12's legal forward pass is thrown to A88 who is near the sideline and catches the ball inbounds at the B-20. A88 stepped out of bounds at the B-30 well before the pass was released and immediately reestablished inbounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, 50, Ready. A88 has committed a foul for Illegal Touching. This type of illegal touching carries a penalty of loss of down at the previous spot.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. A12's legal forward pass is thrown to A88 who was inbounds for the entire play and is near the sideline when he catches the ball inbounds at the B-20. A88 was lined up at tight end and was covered up by wide receiver A80.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, A-45, Ready. Because A88 is on the line of scrimmage but is not the end man on the line, by rule A88 is not an eligible receiver. No originally ineligible receiver may intentionally touch a legal forward pass until it has touched an opponent or an official. This type of Illegal Touching carries a penalty of 5 yards from the previous spot, plus loss of down.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. Eligible receiver A44 is running a pass pattern near the sideline. As a legal forward pass comes toward him, he accidentally steps on the sideline, leaps from out of bounds, muffs the pass into the air while airborne, returns to the ground inbounds, grabs the ball and lands on his knees inbounds with the ball firmly in his possession at the B-20.
choice_b: A, 2/10, 50, Snap. Illegal Touching on A44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, 50, Snap. A44 first touched the ball while airborne and thus out of bounds since he had not reestablished inbounds, so the ball is out of bounds and the pass is incomplete. There is no foul for Illegal Touching.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter, quarterback A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the A-45 that falls incomplete after ineligible A58 touches the ball at the B-49 in an attempt to catch it. There was no eligible receiver in the area.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, A-45, 10-Second Runoff. This is a foul for intentional grounding; there is no foul for illegal touching since the pass is illegal. The penalty is loss of down at the spot of the pass. Because this play occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, this foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. QB A12 drops back to pass and throws downfield but the pass is blocked by B55. A12 catches the blocked pass at the A-45 and immediately throws to A88 who makes the catch at the B-30 and is downed inbounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/20, A-40, Ready. The catch by A12 is legal, but when he throws a second forward pass during the down, that is a foul for an illegal forward pass. The 5-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul and also carries a loss of down.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. B21 intercepts A12's legal forward pass at the B-4. His momentum is taking him into the end zone when he fumbles the ball at the B-1. The ball rolls into the end zone, and B45 recovers the ball while grounded in the end zone.
choice_b: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-25, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules. The momentum exception does not apply because all conditions of the exception are not satisfied. B21's fumble is responsible for the ball being in Team B's end zone, and the ball becomes dead in the possession of B45, resulting in a safety.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98's kick is rolling on the ground at the B-3 when B10 kicks the ball into the end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, Re-kick, A-45, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-25, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, Re-kick, A-45, FK Rules. There is a new impetus by B10 when he kicks the ball into and out of the end zone, so the result of the play is a safety. B10 commits a foul for illegally kicking the ball; if the 10-yard penalty is accepted, it is enforced from the previous spot and the kick repeated.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A98's kick is successful and B55 was offside at the snap. After the ball is dead, B55 then commits a personal foul.
choice_a: Score 1 point for Team A and A, Free Kick @ 35, FK Rules.
choice_b: A, Try @ 3/4 yard line, No Clock -- or -- Score 1 point for Team A and A, Free Kick @ 50, FK Rules.
choice_d: Score 2 points for Team A and A, Free Kick @ 50, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Try @ 3/4 yard line, No Clock -- or -- Score 1 point for Team A and A, Free Kick @ 50, FK Rules. If Team A elects to replay the try, both penalties against Team B are enforced before the snap. If Team A declines the offside penalty and accepts the point, the penalty for Team B's dead-ball foul is then enforced on the kickoff.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QB A1 scrambles to the B-35 where he is tackled by B4, and the Line Judge throws a flag. The Referee announces, "personal foul - late hit with targeting". Replays show A1 diving head first and B4 making forcible contact with his shoulder into the side of A1's helmet before he was down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-20, Ready. A1 is not defenseless since he is not sliding feet first or clearly on the ground prior to the contact, so only the crown-of-helmet standard applies. The contact with the shoulder is not targeting, and B4 remains in the game. The late hit penalty is not reviewable and is enforced from the end of the run.

question_text: 2/7 @ B-8. Early in the 2nd quarter, back A2 runs near the sideline to the B-5 where he fumbles the ball, and it goes out of bounds at the B-8. The officials spot the ball at the B-6.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/7, B-8, Ready. The spot where the fumble goes out of bounds is reviewable regardless of whether the line to gain is involved. The two-yard difference is significant inside the 10-yard line, but replay should use O2O to correct the spot in lieu of a full review.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-5 late in the second quarter. Quarterback A11 drops back to pass and is scrambling in his end zone as he tries to find an open receiver. About to be tackled in the end zone, A11 throws the ball forward to the ground in an area where there are no eligible receivers. The referee throws a flag for intentional grounding. When the ball is dead the game clock shows 0:18. Team B accepts the penalty.
choice_a: Safety, Two points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, 10-Second Runoff.
choice_b: Safety, Two points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, Ready.
choice_c: A, 1/10, A-20, Free Kick, No Clock.
choice_d: Safety, Two points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-35, 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, Two points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, 10-Second Runoff. A11's action is intentional grounding, and the spot of the foul is in the end zone, resulting in a safety. Team A will free kick at the A-20. Team B has the option for a 10-second runoff.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the game with the score tied, Kicker A98 places the ball down at the right hash for an apparent onside kick. After the Referee's ready-for-play signal, A98 approaches the ball and then picks up the ball, runs to the left hash at the A-35, places the ball down and quickly kicks the ball. The ball is recovered by A21 at the A-47.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. By interpretation, once the ball position between the hash marks has been selected by Team A and after the ready-for-play, the ball may be relocated only after a charged team timeout or before any re-kick. The action by A98 results in a dead-ball foul, and the penalty is enforced five yards from the succeeding spot.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-10. A86 is legally blocked out of bounds by B18 at Team B's two-yard line. A86, while attempting an immediate return to the field of play, leaps from out of bounds and is airborne as he receives A16's legal forward pass. He lands in Team B's end zone with the ball in his grasp.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-10, Snap. A86 did not lose his eligibility since he returned inbounds immediately after being forced out by contact with the opponent. However, the pass is incomplete because A86 did not establish inbounds before touching the pass, so he was still out of bounds when he touched it.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A98's kick is successful, but B54 is lined up offside. After the ball is dead, Team B commits a dead-ball personal foul.
choice_c: Score 2 points for Team A and A, Free Kick, 50, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Re-Try, B-3/4, No Clock -- or -- A, Free Kick, 50, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Re-Try, B-3/4, No Clock -- or -- A, Free Kick, 50, FK Rules. If Team A elects to replay the try, both penalties against Team B are enforced before the snap. If Team A declines the offside penalty and accepts the point, the penalty for Team B's dead-ball foul is enforced on the succeeding kickoff or at the succeeding spot in extra periods.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. Shotgun Quarterback A8 takes the snap and runs around the right side with A34 running side-by-side. At the A-29, A8 thrusts the ball laterally toward A34 who touches the ball at the A-30. A8 releases the ball at A-31 at which time A34's hands are slightly forward of A8.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/14, A-26, Ready. This is a foul for illegal forward handing. The spot of the foul is where A34 gained control of the ball, the A-31. The penalty is five yards from the spot of the foul and includes loss of down.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A12 retreats and then passes forward to A21 who is the intended receiver and is in the area. Lineman A70, while in his end zone, reaches out and touches the ball as he attempts to catch the pass, and the pass falls incomplete.
choice_d: Safety, 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/13, A-3, Snap. A70's action is a foul for illegal touching. Team B accepts the five-yard penalty, enforced from the previous spot, which includes loss of down. The pass is legal since A21 was in the area.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A12 retreats and then passes forward to A21 who is the intended receiver and is in the area. Lineman A70, while in his end zone, reaches out and catches the pass and is downed in the end zone.
choice_b: Safety, Score 2 points for Team B w/ A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, Score 2 points for Team B w/ A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, 4/13, A-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, Score 2 points for Team B w/ A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, 4/13, A-3, Snap. After the catch by A70, when he is downed the ball is dead in the end zone with impetus by Team A, so the result of the play is a safety. The action by A70 is illegal touching, and Team B may instead elect the penalty enforced at the previous spot with loss of down. The pass is legal since A21 was in the area.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-6. QB A12 retreats and then passes forward to A21 who is the intended receiver and is in the area. Lineman A70, while in his end zone, reaches out and catches the pass and is downed after having advanced to his two-yard line.
choice_c: Safety, Score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/14, A-2, Ready. A70 commits a foul for illegal touching. If accepted, the five-yard penalty would be enforced from the previous spot with loss of down, leaving the ball at the A-3 and 4th down. Since the result of the play (downed at the 2-yard line) is more advantageous to Team B than the penalty, they decline the illegal touching penalty and the down counts.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A1's punt bounces just past returner B3 at the B-20 and towards Team B's goal line. Thinking the ball touched B3, both B14 and A8 attempt to recover it at the B-15. A8 ends up with the ball on the ground there. The officials rule B3 touched the ball and give Team A possession. Replays show B3 did not touch the ball, but that B14 touched it before A8 recovered.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. The touching of the kick is reviewable, and since Team B touched it first, it is Team A's ball. Had this been a coach's challenge, it would be successful since a reviewable aspect of the play was changed, even though the final result is the same.

question_text: 4/3 @ A-37. A1's punt is partially blocked and the ball lands at the A-38 before rebounding back to the A-34 where it is recovered on the ground by A56. There is a flag on the play and the Referee announces holding on Team B during the kick at the A-40, and since the ball did not cross the line of scrimmage the penalty is enforced from the previous spot resulting in a 1st down for Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, 50, Snap. Whether the kick crossed the line of scrimmage is reviewable, and since it did, postscrimmage kick enforcement rules apply. The 10-yard penalty is enforced from the end of the kick or the spot of the foul, whichever is farther back, and Team B gets possession.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. Team A is in formation to kick a field goal and Team B has 11 players in its formation. Just before the ball is snapped a 12th Team B player runs onto the field. The ball is snapped, and Team A's field goal is successful.
choice_a: Score 3 points for Team A with A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules -- or -- A, 1/10, B-20, Snap.
choice_c: Score 3 points for Team A with A, Free Kick, A-40, FK Rules.
choice_d: Score 3 points for Team A with A, Free Kick, A-35, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 3 points for Team A with A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules -- or -- A, 1/10, B-20, Snap. The substitution infraction is a live ball foul. If the penalty is accepted, it is penalized from the previous spot, the B-25, giving Team A a first down. Team A can instead decline the penalty and accept the 3 points.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. The ball is ready for play with the play clock and game clock running, and the umpire (or center judge) is in his regular position. Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second and snaps the ball. The umpire (or center judge) is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. A44 gains 5 yards on a quick handoff up the middle.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. The play is shut down and officials work to prevent any live ball action. The game clock is stopped, and the defense is allowed to substitute in response to Team A's late substitutions. No foul this time; the play clock is set to 25 seconds, and both clocks start on the ready-for-play signal. The Referee informs the Team A head coach that any subsequent such actions will result in an unsportsmanlike conduct foul.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter and both teams are out of timeouts. Team A, behind by 5 points, completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. Just after the play, B27 falls to the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:05 on the game clock.
rule_refs: 2024 NCAA Football Play Interpretations, Bulletin No. 2
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, 10-Second Runoff. Based on the new interpretation, if a player injury occurs on a play with a temporary clock stoppage for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters, the opponent has the option of a 10-second runoff. Here Team A, as the non-fouling team benefiting from the stoppage, will decline the runoff option and the clock will start on the snap.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter and both teams are out of timeouts. Team A, behind by 5 points, completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. Just after the play, B27 falls to the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:15 on the game clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Because Team A made the line to gain and the clock was stopped to award a first down, there is no option for a 10-second runoff. The clock will start on the Referee's signal following the injury timeout. The new interpretation does not apply since there are not 10 seconds or less remaining.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter and both teams are out of timeouts. Team A, behind by 5 points, completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. A88 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:05 on the game clock.
rule_refs: 2024 NCAA Football Play Interpretations, Bulletin No. 2
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, 10-Second Runoff. Based on the new interpretation, if a player injury occurs on a play with a temporary clock stoppage for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters, the opponent has the option of a 10-second runoff. Here it is Team A's own player injured, so Team B has the option of the runoff, and the game is over if Team B exercises it.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. It is late in the 4th quarter and both teams are out of timeouts. Team A, behind by 5 points, completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. At the snap, Team A's formation was not legal. A88 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:05 remaining on the game clock.
rule_refs: 2024 NCAA Football Play Interpretations, Bulletin No. 2, Play #2(b)
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15, B-35, Ready. There is no option for a 10-second runoff since the official's timeout includes addressing the Illegal Formation foul by Team A. The new interpretation does not apply when the fouling team is the one that benefited from the stoppage.

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. A98 punts the ball and B21 settles under the ball at the B-15 to receive the punt, but muffs the punt and it rolls free. A80, in an attempt to recover the ball, accidentally kicks it at the B-16 and the ball rolls back into Team B's end zone. There is a scramble for the ball, and B21 recovers in his own end zone.
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B, Free Kick, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. There is no foul for A80's kick of the ball since the act is not intentional. This accidental kick does not change the status of the ball; the initial impetus, A98's punt, is responsible for the ball being in the end zone. Since Team A's punt is responsible for the ball being there, it is a touchback.

question_text: 2/5 @ A-48. Quarterback A12 drops back in the pocket and sets up to pass. Just as he releases the ball he is hit by defensive end B98 who drives his shoulder into A12's knee. The pass is caught by A84 who is tackled after a 12-yard gain to the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Ready. B98 commits a foul for roughing the passer by forcibly hitting the passer in the knee area or below while the passer is in a passing posture. The 15-yard penalty is enforced from the B-40, the end of the run by A84.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-35. B13 intercepts A1's forward pass at the B-20 and returns it down the sideline. He drops the ball as he crosses the goal line and the officials immediately signal TD. Replays show the ball loose just short of the goal line and it comes to rest in the end zone with no player from either team attempting to recover it.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/Goal, A-1, Snap. Whether the ball broke the plane of the goal line in B13's possession is reviewable. Since it came loose before breaking the plane, it is a fumble, and without a clear recovery in the continuing action, the ball comes back to the spot where B13 lost possession.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-45. With 4:30 remaining in the 4th quarter, Team A trails 45-0. QB A1 is hit as he attempts to pass, and the ball goes forward to the B-45 where B55 dives to try and catch it. B55 comes up with the ball and the officials rule fumble recovered by the defense. While the officials are waiting to make the ball ready for play, the RO cycles through several angles but there is nothing definitive on whether the QB fumbled or whether B55 caught the ball before it hit the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-45, Snap. QB pass/fumble and whether B55 caught the ball are both reviewable aspects of the play. Given the score and time remaining, replay should not stop the game here, as the evidence is not definitive enough to constitute an obvious mistake.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. A12 throws a Touchdown pass to A88 who drops the ball in the end zone, and quickly celebrates with teammates. As A88 is leaving the end zone, he simulates firing a machine gun.
choice_a: No penalty. Touchdown for Team A w/ A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A w/ either A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules -- or -- A, Try, B-18, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A w/ either A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, Try, B-18, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A w/ either A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, Try, B-18, No Clock. It is an automatic unsportsmanlike conduct foul if a player simulates the firing of any weapon, including assault rifles, shotguns, machine guns, or handguns. The touchdown counts, and the penalty is enforced on the try or the succeeding kickoff, at Team A's choice.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. A12 throws a Touchdown pass to A88 who drops the ball in the end zone, and quickly celebrates with teammates. After the play, A12, back from where he released the pass, pulls handguns from a pair of imaginary holsters and begins firing.
choice_a: No penalty. Touchdown for Team A w/ A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A w/ either A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules -- or -- A, Try, B-18, No Clock.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A w/ either A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, Try, B-18, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A w/ either A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules -- or -- A, Try, B-18, No Clock. It is an automatic unsportsmanlike conduct foul if a player simulates the firing of any weapon. The touchdown counts, and the penalty is enforced on the try or the succeeding kickoff, at Team A's choice, the same treatment regardless of which player commits the act.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. Late in the game Team A, out of timeouts, makes a first down, stopping the clock which reads 0:03. Team A intends to spike the ball and run an additional play. The referee appropriately blows his whistle and signals, which starts the game clock. The quarterback takes the snap and raises the ball high over his head before throwing it directly to the ground. The game clock shows 0:00.
choice_a: Game over, the clock has expired.
choice_d: Team A penalized for delay of game; Game over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Game over, the clock has expired. Time in the game has expired. Although there were 3 seconds on the game clock when the referee signaled it to start, there is no guarantee of enough time to run an additional play beyond the spike. The offense must execute the spike in a timely manner and the ball must hit the ground with time remaining for the offense to get another play.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50. Late in the game Team A, out of timeouts, makes a first down, stopping the clock which reads 0:02. Team A intends to spike the ball and run an additional play.
